title: "UKALC_primary"
date: "Last compiled on `r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document: 
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    fig_caption: true
    code_folding: show
---

# UKALC Primary Data Preparation Pipeline

## Overview

Prepares CLEAN datasets for R01AA027990: "Estradiol Effects on Behavioral and Reward Sensitivity to Alcohol across the Menstrual Cycle" (PIs: Mark Fillmore & Michelle Martel, University of Kentucky).

**Pipeline:** Import → Clean → Merge surveys (AM/PM) → Integrate cycle data → Merge hormone assays → Generate diagnostics → Save clean output

**Key Outputs:** `ukalc_YYYYMMDD_clean.rds` (clean dataset with NO derived variables), diagnostic plots

**IMPORTANT CHANGE:** This file now outputs clean data ONLY (no rolling averages, person-standardization, or other derived variables). Derived variables are created in UKALC_03_processing_shared.Rmd AFTER merging with ADHD data to ensure both datasets receive identical processing.


```{r setup}
# Load packages, set options, create output folder
source("UKALC_00_setup.R")

# Create subfolder for data prep outputs
data_prep_folder <- file.path(output_folder, "01_data_preparation")
if (!dir.exists(data_prep_folder)) {
  dir.create(data_prep_folder, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("Created subfolder:", data_prep_folder, "\n")
}
```

```{r source-custom-fxs, quiet=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load helper functions from scripts/
source("scripts/plot_day_of_week.R")
source("scripts/print_variable_names.R")
source("scripts/count_rows_ids.R")
source("scripts/standardize_tubenumber.R")

cat("Custom functions sourced.\n")
```

## Data Import

```{r import-survey-data}
# Import AM and PM surveys from REDCap (includes historical Qualtrics data)
# Handles UTF-8 BOM, standardizes column names, removes blank rows

# Define Box folder path (UPDATE THIS for your machine)
data_path <- "~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/02_datasets/UKALC_DAILYSURVEYS/02_data_prep_workspace"

# Helper function: Load CSV, clean column names, standardize ID
load_and_clean_survey <- function(filename) {
  
  # Read CSV with UTF-8 encoding (handles BOM)
  df <- read_csv(
    file.path(data_path, filename), 
    show_col_types = FALSE,
    locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8"),
    name_repair = "unique_quiet"
  )
  
  # Strip UTF-8 BOM from column names (\ufeffid → id)
  names(df) <- sub("^\\ufeff", "", names(df))
  
  # Standardize all column names to snake_case (MoodSwing → mood_swing)
  df <- df %>% janitor::clean_names()
  
  # Remove empty columns
  df <- df %>%
    select(-matches("^x\\d+$")) %>%  # Remove auto-named columns like "x1", "x2"
    select(where(~ !all(is.na(.))))   # Remove columns with only NA values
  
  # Ensure 'id' column exists after cleaning
  # janitor::clean_names() may have renamed it, so check common variants
  if (!"id" %in% names(df)) {
    # Check for common id column names after cleaning
    id_candidates <- c("subject_id", "participant_id", "record_id", "user_id", "patient_id")
    id_col <- intersect(id_candidates, names(df))
    if (length(id_col) > 0) {
      df <- df %>% rename(id = !!sym(id_col[1]))
      cat(sprintf("  Renamed '%s' to 'id'\n", id_col[1]))
    } else {
      stop(sprintf("Cannot find 'id' column in %s after cleaning. Available columns: %s", 
                   filename, paste(names(df), collapse=", ")))
    }
  }
  
  # Keep only digits in 'id' column (handles various ID formats like "UKALC_101" -> "101")
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(id = str_extract(as.character(id), "\\d+"))
  
  # Remove completely empty rows (where id is NA/empty AND all other non-id columns are NA or whitespace)
  # Count rows before filtering
  n_rows_before <- nrow(df)
  
  df <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(id) & id != "")
  
  # Report removed rows
  n_removed <- n_rows_before - nrow(df)
  if (n_removed > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  Removed %d rows with missing/empty id from %s\n", n_removed, filename))
  }
  
  # Standardize hormone column names (E2, P4) - inlined from rename_id_E2_P4.R
  # Standardize estradiol/estrogen-related column names to start with "e2"
  # Only rename columns where the hormone term is the leading identifier (e.g., "estradiol_ng_ml"),
  # avoiding changes to names where it appears later (e.g., "phase2_estrogen_level").
  df <- df %>%
    rename_with(
      ~ gsub("^(estrogen|estradiol|e2)(_?)", "e2\\2", ., ignore.case = TRUE),
      matches("^(estrogen|estradiol|e2)($|_)", ignore.case = TRUE)
    )
  
  # Standardize progesterone-related column names to start with "p4"
  # Only rename columns where the hormone term is the leading identifier.
  df <- df %>%
    rename_with(
      ~ gsub("^(progesterone|p4)(_?)", "p4\\2", ., ignore.case = TRUE),
      matches("^(progesterone|p4)($|_)", ignore.case = TRUE)
    )
  
  # Convert id to factor
  df <- df %>% mutate(id = as.factor(id))
  
  return(df)
}

# ---- 2. Load & Process Datasets ----

am_rc   <- load_and_clean_survey("AM_REDCap_csv.csv")
pm_rc   <- load_and_clean_survey("PM_REDCap_csv.csv")

# ---- 3. Diagnostics ----

# Print quick summary of unique IDs per dataset
datasets <- list(AM_REDCap = am_rc, PM_REDCap = pm_rc)

cat("\n--- Processing Complete ---\n")
for (name in names(datasets)) {
  n_ids <- n_distinct(datasets[[name]]$id)
  n_rows <- nrow(datasets[[name]])
  cat(sprintf("%s: %d rows, %d unique IDs\n", name, n_rows, n_ids))
}

```

## Clean AM Survey Data

```{r clean-am-rc, warning=FALSE}
# Clean morning surveys: alcohol use, cravings, stress, medications
# Parses dates, standardizes craving names (crav_* → crave_*_am), validates 0-10 scale
# Imputes num_drinks=0 when craving reported but drinks missing

# ---- 1. Process Dates ----
# NOTE: janitor::clean_names() has already been applied in load_and_clean_survey()
# so date_time, tube_number, date_rated are already lowercase

# Parse date_time column to create daterated, handling blank/NA values safely
am_rc <- am_rc %>%
  mutate(
    # Only parse non-empty, non-NA date_time values
    daterated = if_else(
      !is.na(date_time) & date_time != "" & !str_detect(date_time, "^\\s*$"),
      as.Date(suppressWarnings(lubridate::mdy_hm(date_time))),
      as.Date(NA_character_)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-date_time)

# ---- 1a. Remove Pre-existing Cleaned Columns (if any) ----
# Some CSV files may contain pre-cleaned versions of alcohol timing columns
# (e.g., drink_time_start_clean, drink_dur_hrs) from external processing.
# Remove these to avoid duplicates, as we will generate our own cleaned versions.
pre_cleaned_cols <- c("drink_time_start_clean", "drink_dur_hrs")
existing_pre_cleaned <- intersect(pre_cleaned_cols, names(am_rc))

if (length(existing_pre_cleaned) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Removing Pre-existing Cleaned Columns ===\n"))
  cat(sprintf("Found %d pre-cleaned columns in CSV: %s\n", 
              length(existing_pre_cleaned), 
              paste(existing_pre_cleaned, collapse=", ")))
  cat("These will be removed and regenerated using standardized text parsing functions.\n")
  
  am_rc <- am_rc %>%
    select(-any_of(pre_cleaned_cols))
}

# ---- 1b. Standardize Craving Column Names ----
# NOTE: janitor::clean_names() has converted crav_alc_AM -> crav_alc_am already
# Now we just need to change "crav_" prefix to "crave_"
am_rc <- am_rc %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("^crav_", "crave_", .), starts_with("crav_"))

# Validate transformation: Check if any old-format craving columns remain
remaining_crav <- names(am_rc)[grepl("^crav_", names(am_rc), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(remaining_crav) > 0) {
  warning(sprintf("AM: Found %d craving columns that were not transformed: %s", 
                  length(remaining_crav), paste(remaining_crav, collapse = ", ")))
}

# ---- 1c. Track Missing Data BEFORE Filtering ----
# Document missing data patterns before removing any rows
n_total_rows <- nrow(am_rc)
n_missing_id <- sum(is.na(am_rc$id))
n_missing_daterated <- sum(is.na(am_rc$daterated))
n_missing_both <- sum(is.na(am_rc$id) & is.na(am_rc$daterated))

cat("\n=== AM Survey Missing Data Report (Pre-filtering) ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Total rows: %d\n", n_total_rows))
cat(sprintf("Rows with missing id: %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_missing_id, 100 * n_missing_id / n_total_rows))
cat(sprintf("Rows with missing daterated: %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_missing_daterated, 100 * n_missing_daterated / n_total_rows))
cat(sprintf("Rows with both missing: %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_missing_both, 100 * n_missing_both / n_total_rows))

# ---- 1d. Filter Rows with Missing Keys ----
# Drop observations where id or daterated are missing (cannot be matched in merge)
am_rc <- am_rc %>%
  filter(!is.na(id), !is.na(daterated))

cat(sprintf("Rows after removing missing id/daterated: %d (removed %d rows)\n\n", 
            nrow(am_rc), n_total_rows - nrow(am_rc)))

# ---- 2. Clean Alcohol Variables ----
# Convert drank_alcohol to factor (1/0)
am_rc <- am_rc %>%
  mutate(drank_alcohol = as.factor(drank_alcohol))

# Ensure num_drinks is numeric
am_rc <- am_rc %>% 
  mutate(num_drinks = as.numeric(num_drinks))

# ---- 2a. Text Parsing Functions for Alcohol Duration and Start Time ----
# These functions clean free-text responses from participants about drinking timing
# Adapted from archive/old_scripts/UKALC_textparse.Rmd

#' Clean Time String to HH:MM Format
#'
#' Converts free-text time entries (e.g., "7pm", "around 9:30", "10") to 24-hour HH:MM format.
#' Handles common variations including AM/PM indicators, words like "noon"/"midnight",
#' and missing/invalid entries.
#'
#' @param x Character string representing a time (e.g., "7pm", "21:30", "noon")
#' @return Character string in HH:MM format (24-hour), or NA if unparseable
#'
#' @examples
#' clean_time_single("7pm")        # Returns "19:00"
#' clean_time_single("around 9:30") # Returns "21:30" (assumes PM if no indicator)
#' clean_time_single("noon")       # Returns "12:00"
#' clean_time_single("midnight")   # Returns "00:00"
#' clean_time_single("no drink")   # Returns NA
clean_time_single <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x) || x == "") return(NA_character_)
  x <- str_to_lower(str_trim(x))
  
  # Handle explicit "no drinking" responses
  if (x %in% c("0", "nan", "none", "na") || str_detect(x, "no drink|didn't|consume")) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }
  
  # Handle special keywords
  if (str_detect(x, "noon")) return("12:00")
  if (str_detect(x, "midnight")) return("00:00")
  
  # Remove ranges and qualifiers, keep first time mentioned
  x <- str_trim(str_split(x, "[-/]| and | for ")[[1]][1])
  x <- str_remove_all(x, "around|at|approx|start|time|about")
  x <- str_trim(x)
  
  # Extract time pattern: digits with optional minutes and AM/PM
  pattern <- "(\\d{1,2})(?:[:.](\\d{2}))?\\s*([ap]\\.?m?\\.?)?"
  match <- str_match(x, pattern)
  
  if (is.na(match[1,1])) return(NA_character_)
  
  h <- as.integer(match[1,2])
  m <- as.integer(match[1,3])
  if (is.na(m)) m <- 0
  ampm <- match[1,4]
  
  # Convert to 24-hour format
  if (!is.na(ampm) && ampm != "") {
    ampm <- str_remove_all(ampm, "\\.")
    if (str_detect(ampm, "p") && h < 12) h <- h + 12
    if (str_detect(ampm, "a") && h == 12) h <- 0
  } else {
    # If no AM/PM indicator, assume PM for drinking hours (evening bias)
    if (h <= 11 && h != 0) h <- h + 12
    if (h == 24) h <- 0
  }
  
  # Validate time bounds
  if (h > 23 || m > 59) return(NA_character_)
  
  return(sprintf("%02d:%02d", h, m))
}

#' Clean Duration String to Numeric Hours
#'
#' Converts free-text duration entries (e.g., "2-3 hours", "1h 30m", "90 minutes") 
#' to numeric hours. Handles ranges (takes midpoint), explicit units, and common
#' text variations.
#'
#' @param x Character string representing duration (e.g., "2 hours", "1:30", "90 min")
#' @return Numeric value in hours, or NA if unparseable
#'
#' @examples
#' clean_duration_single("2 hours")    # Returns 2.0
#' clean_duration_single("1h 30m")     # Returns 1.5
#' clean_duration_single("2-3")        # Returns 2.5 (midpoint)
#' clean_duration_single("90 minutes") # Returns 1.5
#' clean_duration_single("1:30")       # Returns 1.5
#' clean_duration_single("no drink")   # Returns 0.0
clean_duration_single <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x) || x == "") return(NA_real_)
  x <- str_to_lower(str_trim(x))
  
  # Handle explicit "no drinking" responses - return 0
  if (x %in% c("0", "nan", "none", "na", "no") || 
      str_detect(x, "no drink|didn't|consume|zero")) {
    return(0.0)
  }
  
  # Reject timestamps mistakenly entered as duration (e.g., "4pm")
  if (str_detect(x, "[0-9] ?[ap]\\.?m")) return(NA_real_)
  
  # Standardize text to numeric
  x <- x %>%
    str_replace_all("one", "1") %>% str_replace_all("two", "2") %>%
    str_replace_all("three", "3") %>% str_replace_all("four", "4") %>%
    str_replace_all("five", "5") %>% str_replace_all("half", "0.5") %>%
    str_replace_all("an hour", "1 hour") %>% str_replace_all("a minute", "1 minute") %>%
    str_replace_all("1/2", "0.5") %>%
    str_replace_all("hrs|hr|hours|hour", "h") %>%
    str_replace_all("mins|min|minutes|minute", "m")
  
  # Handle Range Pattern (e.g., "3-4" or "2 to 3") - take midpoint
  range_match <- str_match(x, "^(\\d+(\\.\\d+)?)\\s*[-to]+\\s*(\\d+(\\.\\d+)?)$")
  if (!is.na(range_match[1,1])) {
    return((as.numeric(range_match[1,2]) + as.numeric(range_match[1,4])) / 2)
  }
  
  # Handle Explicit Unit Pattern (e.g., "1h 30m")
  h_val <- 0; m_val <- 0; found_unit <- FALSE
  h_match <- str_match(x, "(\\d+(\\.\\d+)?)\\s*h")
  if (!is.na(h_match[1,1])) { h_val <- as.numeric(h_match[1,2]); found_unit <- TRUE }
  m_match <- str_match(x, "(\\d+(\\.\\d+)?)\\s*m")
  if (!is.na(m_match[1,1])) { m_val <- as.numeric(m_match[1,2]); found_unit <- TRUE }
  if (found_unit) return(h_val + (m_val / 60))
  
  # Handle Colon Pattern (e.g., "1:30" as 1 hour 30 minutes)
  colon_match <- str_match(x, "^(\\d{1,2}):(\\d{2})$")
  if (!is.na(colon_match[1,1])) {
    h <- as.numeric(colon_match[1,2]); m <- as.numeric(colon_match[1,3])
    if (h <= 12) return(h + (m / 60))
  }
  
  # Handle Bare Number (assume hours if < 15, minutes if >= 15)
  num_clean <- str_remove_all(x, "[^0-9\\.]")
  if (num_clean != "" && !is.na(as.numeric(num_clean))) {
    val <- as.numeric(num_clean)
    if (val >= 15) return(val / 60) else return(val)
  }
  
  return(NA_real_)
}

# ---- 2b. Apply Text Parsing to Alcohol Variables ----
# Clean time_start_alcohol and dur_alcohol using the parsing functions
cat("\n=== Cleaning Alcohol Duration and Start Time ===\n")

# Count non-missing values before cleaning
n_start_before <- sum(!is.na(am_rc$time_start_alcohol))
n_dur_before <- sum(!is.na(am_rc$dur_alcohol))

# Apply cleaning functions
am_rc <- am_rc %>%
  mutate(
    time_start_alcohol_clean = map_chr(time_start_alcohol, clean_time_single),
    dur_alcohol_hours = map_dbl(dur_alcohol, clean_duration_single)
  )

# Count non-missing values after cleaning
n_start_after <- sum(!is.na(am_rc$time_start_alcohol_clean))
n_dur_after <- sum(!is.na(am_rc$dur_alcohol_hours))

cat(sprintf("time_start_alcohol: %d non-missing → %d cleaned (%.1f%% success rate)\n",
            n_start_before, n_start_after, 
            100 * n_start_after / max(n_start_before, 1)))
cat(sprintf("dur_alcohol: %d non-missing → %d cleaned (%.1f%% success rate)\n",
            n_dur_before, n_dur_after,
            100 * n_dur_after / max(n_dur_before, 1)))

# Show example conversions for validation
if (n_start_before > 0) {
  cat("\nExample time conversions:\n")
  examples_time <- am_rc %>%
    filter(!is.na(time_start_alcohol)) %>%
    select(time_start_alcohol, time_start_alcohol_clean) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    head(5)
  print(examples_time)
}

if (n_dur_before > 0) {
  cat("\nExample duration conversions:\n")
  examples_dur <- am_rc %>%
    filter(!is.na(dur_alcohol)) %>%
    select(dur_alcohol, dur_alcohol_hours) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    head(5)
  print(examples_dur)
}

# ---- Quality Check: Validate Parsed Values ----
# Check for unreasonable duration values that may indicate parsing errors
extreme_durations <- am_rc %>%
  filter(!is.na(dur_alcohol_hours)) %>%
  filter(dur_alcohol_hours > 12 | dur_alcohol_hours < 0) %>%
  select(id, daterated, dur_alcohol, dur_alcohol_hours)

if (nrow(extreme_durations) > 0) {
  cat("\n⚠️  WARNING: Found", nrow(extreme_durations), "drinking episodes with unusual duration (>12 hours or <0):\n")
  print(extreme_durations)
  cat("Review these values manually to ensure they were parsed correctly.\n")
}

# Check for extreme number of drinks that may be data entry errors
extreme_drinks <- am_rc %>%
  filter(!is.na(num_drinks_today)) %>%
  filter(num_drinks_today > 20) %>%
  select(id, daterated, num_drinks_today)

if (nrow(extreme_drinks) > 0) {
  cat("\n⚠️  WARNING: Found", nrow(extreme_drinks), "drinking episodes with >20 drinks:\n")
  print(extreme_drinks)
  cat("These values may be data entry errors. Review and consider capping if implausible.\n")
}

# ---- 3. Clean Craving Variables ----
# Craving columns have been standardized to: crave_alc_am, crave_nic_am, crave_mar_am, etc.
# (renamed from crav_*_AM to crave_*_am in section 1b above)
# NOTE: The _am suffix is already present from section 1b

# Record NA rates before transformation
crave_cols_pre <- names(am_rc)[grepl("^crave_.*_am$", names(am_rc), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(crave_cols_pre) > 0) {
  crave_pre <- am_rc %>% 
    summarise(across(matches("^crave_.*_am$"), ~ mean(is.na(.))))
  cat("AM Craving Variables NA proportions (before cleaning):\n")
  print(crave_pre)
}

# Clean craving variables - extract leading digits if text format present
# Handles formats like: "5 Moderate Urge", "10", 10
am_rc <- am_rc %>%
  mutate(across(matches("^crave_.*_am$"), ~ suppressWarnings(as.integer(stringr::str_extract(as.character(.), "^\\d+")))))

# Record NA rates after transformation
if (length(crave_cols_pre) > 0) {
  crave_post <- am_rc %>% 
    summarise(across(matches("^crave_.*_am$"), ~ mean(is.na(.))))
  cat("\nAM Craving Variables NA proportions (after cleaning):\n")
  print(crave_post)
  
  # Report if cleaning introduced NAs
  for(col in crave_cols_pre) {
    na_before <- crave_pre[[col]]
    na_after <- crave_post[[col]]
    if(na_after > na_before) {
      cat(sprintf("WARNING: %s had %.1f%% NAs increase from %.1f%% to %.1f%% due to text extraction\n", 
                  col, 100*(na_after - na_before), 100*na_before, 100*na_after))
    }
  }
}

# Validate craving values are in expected range (0-10)
crave_cols <- names(am_rc)[grepl("^crave_.*_am$", names(am_rc), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(crave_cols) > 0) {
  for (col in crave_cols) {
    out_of_range <- am_rc[[col]][!is.na(am_rc[[col]]) & (am_rc[[col]] < 0 | am_rc[[col]] > 10)]
    if (length(out_of_range) > 0) {
      warning(sprintf("AM RC: %s has %d values outside expected 0-10 range", col, length(out_of_range)))
    }
  }
}

# ---- 4. Imputation Logic with Documentation ----
# If crave_alc_am is not missing but num_drinks is missing, impute num_drinks = 0
# RATIONALE: Craving question comes after drinking question in survey flow.
# If participant answered craving, they saw drinking question but didn't report drinks.
# This likely means no drinking occurred (rather than technical skip).
if("crave_alc_am" %in% names(am_rc) && "num_drinks" %in% names(am_rc)) {
  n_imputed <- sum(!is.na(am_rc$crave_alc_am) & is.na(am_rc$num_drinks))
  if(n_imputed > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("\n=== Imputation: Setting num_drinks = 0 for %d rows where crave_alc_am exists but num_drinks is NA ===\n", n_imputed))
    am_rc$num_drinks[!is.na(am_rc$crave_alc_am) & is.na(am_rc$num_drinks)] <- 0
  }
}

# ---- 5. Select Final Columns and Standardize Names ----
# Keep all relevant AM variables
# NOTE: Craving variables already have _am suffix from section 1b (crave_alc_am, crave_nic_am, etc.)

# Standardize TubeNumber using helper function for consistency with hormone data
am_rc <- standardize_tubenumber(am_rc)

am_rc <- am_rc %>% 
  select(id, daterated, 
         # Tube number for hormone data matching
         any_of("TubeNumber"),
         # Alcohol variables (raw and cleaned)
         num_drinks, drank_alcohol, felt_drunk, 
         time_start_alcohol, time_start_alcohol_clean,
         dur_alcohol, dur_alcohol_hours,
         # Craving variables (all substances) - with _am suffix
         crave_alc_am, crave_nic_am, crave_mar_am, crave_coc_am, crave_benz_am, 
         crave_hall_am, crave_opiate_am, crave_oth_am,
         # Stress variables
         had_stress, stress_work_school, stress_social, stress_health, 
         stress_financial, stress_logistics, stress_study_related,
         # Medication/drug use variables
         took_meds, drug_pain_nsaid, drug_cold_allergy, drug_antibiotic, 
         drug_psych_sleep, drug_gi, drug_steroid, drug_spironolactone, drug_supplement,
         # Other substance use
         used_nicotine, used_marijuana, used_cocaine, used_benzos, 
         used_hallucinogens, used_opiates)

# ---- 6. Visualization and Diagnostics ----
# Visualize Missing Data
cat("\n=== AM Survey Missing Data Pattern (Final) ===\n")
#vis_miss(am_rc) 

# Print summary
cat("\n=== AM Survey Cleaning Complete ===\n")
cat("Variables: All in snake_case format\n")
cat("Craving variables: crave_alc_am, crave_nic_am, crave_mar_am, etc. (validated 0-10 range, _am suffix already present)\n")
cat("Alcohol variables: num_drinks (numeric), drank_alcohol (factor), felt_drunk\n")
cat("Alcohol timing variables: time_start_alcohol_clean (HH:MM), dur_alcohol_hours (numeric hours)\n")
cat("Stress variables: had_stress, stress_work_school, stress_social, etc.\n")
cat("Medication variables: took_meds, drug_pain_nsaid, drug_cold_allergy, etc.\n")
cat("Substance use: used_nicotine, used_marijuana, used_cocaine, etc.\n\n")

count_rows_ids(am_rc)
```

```{r create-merged-amdf}
# Fill missing dates in AM data: creates row for each day between first/last survey
# Ensures craving variables have "_am" suffix for merge

# Standardize dates and remove empty rows
amdf <- am_rc %>%
  mutate(
    daterated = as_date(daterated),
    id = as.character(id)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(id))

# Fill missing dates (creates row for each day between first and last survey)
amdf_complete <- amdf %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  filter(any(!is.na(daterated))) %>% 
  complete(daterated = seq(min(daterated, na.rm = TRUE), 
                           max(daterated, na.rm = TRUE), 
                           by = "day")) %>%
  ungroup()

# Verify craving variables have "_am" suffix (safety check)
amdf_complete <- amdf_complete %>%
  rename_with(~ ifelse(grepl("_(am|pm)$", .), ., paste0(., "_am")), starts_with("crave_")) %>%
  distinct()

cat("Final AM DataFrame Rows:", nrow(amdf_complete), "\n")
count_rows_ids(amdf_complete)

vis_miss(amdf_complete)
```

## Clean PM Survey Data

```{r clean-pm-rc, warning=FALSE}
# Clean evening surveys: mood (DRSP), impulse control (bdefs), cravings
# Parses dates (handles "before noon" → previous day), standardizes names (crav_* → crave_*_pm)
# Recodes DRSP (1-6 scale) and validates cravings (0-10 scale)
# Note: Temporary datepart/timepart/b4noon kept for duplicate removal, dropped before merge

# ---- 0. Define Variable Lists ----
# These variable lists are needed for recoding DRSP and bdefs scales below
# Following refactoring guidelines: each chunk defines its own variable lists

drsp_vars <- c(
  "dep", "hopeless", "worthless", "anxious", "mood_swing", "rej_sens", 
  "angry", "conflicts", "loss_interest", "diff_conc", "tired", 
  "over_eat", "sp_food_crav", "high_sleep", "diff_sleep", "overwhelm", 
  "loss_control", "breast_tend", "breast_swell", "headache", 
  "jm_pain", "loss_prod", "int_relat"
)

bdefs_vars <- c(
  "diff_inhib", "diff_stop", "diff_change", "immed_payoff", 
  "res_urge", "diff_long_rew", "diff_res_rew"
)

# ---- 1. Process Dates ----
# NOTE: janitor::clean_names() has already been applied in load_and_clean_survey()
# so date_time is already lowercase and ready to use

# Parse date_time column to create daterated
# The raw data has been fixed, so we can now parse dates uniformly
# Handles multiple date formats flexibly using lubridate::parse_date_time
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  mutate(
    # Only parse non-empty, non-NA date_time values
    daterated = if_else(
      ! is.na(date_time) & date_time != "" & ! str_detect(date_time, "^\\s*$"),
      as.Date(coalesce(
        # Try mdy_hms first (handles formats with seconds like "8/12/2021 7:22:00PM")
        suppressWarnings(lubridate::mdy_hms(date_time)),
        # Fall back to mdy_hm (handles "8/30/21 20:14" format without seconds)
        suppressWarnings(lubridate::mdy_hm(date_time))
      )),
      as.Date(NA_character_)
    )
  )


# Save "before noon" completions as day prior
# Handle blank date_time values that would cause str_split to fail
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  mutate(
    date_time_clean = if_else(is.na(date_time) | date_time == "", "___MISSING_DATE___ ___MISSING_TIME___", date_time)
  )

pm_rc[c('datepart', 'timepart')] <- str_split_fixed(pm_rc$date_time_clean, ' ', 2)
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>% select(-date_time, -date_time_clean)

# Parse timepart, handling NA values
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  mutate(
    timepart = suppressWarnings(lubridate::hm(timepart)),
    b4noon = if_else(!is.na(timepart) & timepart < lubridate::hm("12:00"), 1, 0, missing = 0)
  )

pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  mutate(daterated = case_when(
    b4noon == 1 & !is.na(daterated) ~ daterated - 1,
    TRUE ~ daterated
  ))

# ---- 1a. Standardize Craving Column Names ----
# NOTE: janitor::clean_names() has converted crav_alc_PM -> crav_alc_pm already
# Now we just need to change "crav_" prefix to "crave_"
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("^crav_", "crave_", .), starts_with("crav_"))

# Validate transformation: Check if any old-format craving columns remain
remaining_crav <- names(pm_rc)[grepl("^crav_", names(pm_rc), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(remaining_crav) > 0) {
  warning(sprintf("PM: Found %d craving columns that were not transformed: %s", 
                  length(remaining_crav), paste(remaining_crav, collapse = ", ")))
}

# ---- 2. Remove Rows with Blank Data ----
# Identify rows where all non-key fields are NA or empty/whitespace
# Key fields: id, daterated, datepart, timepart, b4noon
# This removes the "blank" rows seen in the PM export

n_total_rows <- nrow(pm_rc)
n_missing_id <- sum(is.na(pm_rc$id))
n_missing_daterated <- sum(is.na(pm_rc$daterated))

cat("\n=== PM Survey Missing Data Report (Pre-filtering) ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Total rows: %d\n", n_total_rows))
cat(sprintf("Rows with missing id: %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_missing_id, 100 * n_missing_id / n_total_rows))
cat(sprintf("Rows with missing daterated: %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_missing_daterated, 100 * n_missing_daterated / n_total_rows))

# Define key columns to exclude from "all NA" check
key_cols <- c("id", "daterated", "datepart", "timepart", "b4noon")
data_cols <- setdiff(names(pm_rc), key_cols)

# Remove rows where:
# 1. id is missing (can't be matched), OR
# 2. All data columns are NA/empty (blank survey row)
# Note: We keep rows with missing daterated if they have actual data,
# as these may have date parsing issues but still contain valid survey responses
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(id) &
    # At least one data column must have a non-NA value
    rowSums(!is.na(select(., all_of(data_cols)))) > 0
  )

n_removed <- n_total_rows - nrow(pm_rc)
cat(sprintf("Rows after removing missing id and blank data rows: %d (removed %d rows)\n", 
            nrow(pm_rc), n_removed))

# Check for rows with missing daterated that were kept
n_kept_missing_daterated <- sum(is.na(pm_rc$daterated))
if (n_kept_missing_daterated > 0) {
  affected_ids <- unique(pm_rc$id[is.na(pm_rc$daterated)])
  cat(sprintf("⚠ WARNING: %d rows have missing daterated but were kept because they contain data\n", 
              n_kept_missing_daterated))
  cat("  These rows may not merge correctly. Investigate date_time values for parsing issues.\n")
  if (length(affected_ids) <= 10) {
    cat("  Affected IDs:", paste(affected_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  } else {
    cat("  Affected IDs (first 10):", paste(head(affected_ids, 10), collapse = ", "), 
        sprintf("... and %d more\n", length(affected_ids) - 10))
  }
  cat("  Next steps:\n")
  cat("  1. Check original date_time format in raw data for these IDs\n")
  cat("  2. Verify date_time values are in 'M/D/YY H:MM' format (e.g., '8/30/21 20:14')\n")
  cat("  3. Look for blank, malformed, or non-standard date strings\n")
}
cat("\n")

# ---- 3. Variable Names Already Cleaned ----
# NOTE: janitor::clean_names() in load_and_clean_survey() has already:
# - Converted Dep -> dep, MoodSwing -> mood_swing, RejSens -> rej_sens
# - Converted Diff_Inhib -> diff_inhib, Diff_Stop -> diff_stop, etc.
# So we don't need manual name mappings anymore!

cat("Variable names already standardized by janitor::clean_names():\n")
cat("  - DRSP: dep, hopeless, mood_swing, rej_sens, etc.\n")
cat("  - bdefs: diff_inhib, diff_stop, diff_change, etc.\n")
cat("  - Craving: crave_alc_pm, crave_nic_pm, etc.\n\n")

# ---- 4. Clean Craving Variables ----
# Craving columns should now be: crave_alc_pm, crave_nic_pm, etc.
# (renamed from crav_* in section 1a above)

# Record NA rates before transformation
crave_cols_pre <- names(pm_rc)[grepl("^crave_.*_pm$", names(pm_rc), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(crave_cols_pre) > 0) {
  crave_pre <- pm_rc %>% 
    summarise(across(matches("^crave_.*_pm$"), ~ mean(is.na(.))))
  cat("\nPM Craving Variables NA proportions (before cleaning):\n")
  print(crave_pre)
}

# Clean craving variables - extract leading digits if text format present
# This handles both:
#   - Numeric values: 0, 1, 2, ..., 10 (kept as-is)
#   - Text labels: "5 Moderate Urge" (extracts the leading "5" → 5)
# The str_extract gets the first digits, as.integer converts to numeric
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  mutate(across(matches("^crave_.*_pm$"), ~ suppressWarnings(as.integer(stringr::str_extract(as.character(.), "^\\d+")))))

# Record NA rates after transformation
if (length(crave_cols_pre) > 0) {
  crave_post <- pm_rc %>% 
    summarise(across(matches("^crave_.*_pm$"), ~ mean(is.na(.))))
  cat("\nPM Craving Variables NA proportions (after cleaning):\n")
  print(crave_post)
}

# Validate craving values are in expected range (0-10)
crave_cols <- names(pm_rc)[grepl("^crave_.*_pm$", names(pm_rc), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(crave_cols) > 0) {
  for (col in crave_cols) {
    out_of_range <- pm_rc[[col]][!is.na(pm_rc[[col]]) & (pm_rc[[col]] < 0 | pm_rc[[col]] > 10)]
    if (length(out_of_range) > 0) {
      warning(sprintf("PM RC: %s has %d values outside expected 0-10 range", col, length(out_of_range)))
    }
  }
}

# ---- 5. Recode DRSP Variables (Only if Character/String) ----
# DRSP uses 6-point scale: Not at All(1) to Extreme(6)
# The example data has numeric values (1-6), so we should preserve those
# Only recode if values are character/string labels

drsp_cols_present <- intersect(drsp_vars, names(pm_rc))
if(length(drsp_cols_present) > 0) {
  # Check data type: numeric values (normal case) or character strings (rare case)
  # Data typically comes in as numeric 1-6, but handle strings just in case
  drsp_is_char <- sapply(pm_rc[drsp_cols_present], function(x) is.character(x) | is.factor(x))
  drsp_char_cols <- names(drsp_is_char)[drsp_is_char]
  
  if(length(drsp_char_cols) > 0) { # Only if strings detected
    cat(sprintf("\nRecoding %d DRSP character variables to numeric scale (1-6)\n", length(drsp_char_cols)))
    
    # Define the recoding as a named vector
    recode_levels <- c(
      "Not at All" = 1, 
      "Minimal" = 2, 
      "Mild" = 3, 
      "Moderate" = 4, 
      "Severe" = 5, 
      "Extreme" = 6
    )
    
    # Identify any unexpected DRSP response levels before recoding
    unexpected_levels <- lapply(pm_rc[drsp_char_cols], function(x) {
      vals <- unique(as.character(x))
      vals[!(is.na(vals) | vals %in% names(recode_levels))]
    })
    cols_with_unexpected <- names(unexpected_levels)[vapply(unexpected_levels, length, integer(1L)) > 0L]
    if (length(cols_with_unexpected) > 0) {
      cat("\nWarning: Unexpected DRSP levels detected in the following variables (will be set to NA):\n")
      for (nm in cols_with_unexpected) {
        cat(sprintf("  - %s: %s\n", nm, paste(unexpected_levels[[nm]], collapse = ", ")))
      }
    }
    
    pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(drsp_char_cols), ~ as.numeric(recode(as.character(.), !!!recode_levels, .default = NA_real_))))
  } else { # No character columns - data is numeric (normal case)
    cat(sprintf("\nDRSP variables (%d found) are already numeric; skipping recoding\n", length(drsp_cols_present)))
  }
  
  # Ensure all DRSP columns are numeric
  pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(drsp_cols_present), ~ as.numeric(.)))
}

# view id, daterated, and diff_inhib columns for checking
# pm_rc %>% select(id, daterated, diff_inhib) %>% View()

# Set bdefs columns to numeric
  
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(bdefs_vars), ~ as.numeric(.)))


# ---- 6a. Standardize TubeNumber (if present) ----
# Use helper function to standardize tube_number to TubeNumber
# This ensures consistency with hormone data and AM survey
pm_rc <- standardize_tubenumber(pm_rc)

# ---- 7. Select Final Columns ----
# Keep all relevant columns
# NOTE: All column names are now in snake_case thanks to janitor::clean_names()
cols_to_keep <- c("id", "daterated", "datepart", "timepart", "b4noon")

# Add TubeNumber if it exists (for hormone data matching)
if ("TubeNumber" %in% names(pm_rc)) {
  cols_to_keep <- c(cols_to_keep, "TubeNumber")
}

# Add bdefs, DRSP, and craving variables using vectorized approach
all_scale_vars <- c(bdefs_vars, drsp_vars, 
                    "crave_alc_pm", "crave_nic_pm", "crave_mar_pm", "crave_coc_pm", 
                    "crave_benzo_pm", "crave_hall_pm", "crave_opiate_pm", "crave_oth_pm")
cols_to_keep <- c(cols_to_keep, intersect(all_scale_vars, names(pm_rc)))

# Add any other description columns that exist
desc_cols <- names(pm_rc)[grepl("_description$", names(pm_rc))]
if(length(desc_cols) > 0) {
  cols_to_keep <- c(cols_to_keep, desc_cols)
}

# Use unique() to ensure no duplicate columns
pm_rc <- pm_rc %>% select(all_of(unique(cols_to_keep)))

# ---- 8. Diagnostics ----
# Visualize Missing Data
cat("\n=== PM Survey Missing Data Pattern (Final) ===\n")
##vis_miss(pm_rc) 

# Print summary
cat("\n=== PM Survey Cleaning Complete ===\n")
cat("Variables: All in snake_case format\n")
cat(sprintf("DRSP variables (%d): dep, hopeless, anxious, mood_swing, etc. (1-6 scale)\n", length(drsp_cols_present)))
cat("Craving variables: crave_alc_pm, crave_nic_pm, crave_mar_pm, etc. (0-10 scale)\n\n")

count_rows_ids(pm_rc)

#vis_miss(pm_rc)

#View(pm_rc)
```

```{r create-merged-pmdf}
# Prepare PM data for merge: remove duplicates, validate structure

pmdf <- pm_rc

# Check for duplicates and remove
pmdf <- pmdf %>%
  distinct()

# Print variable summary
cat("\n=== PM Survey Data Ready for Merge ===\n")
cat("All variables in snake_case with _pm suffix on cravings\n")
cat("bdefs variables (snake_case): diff_inhib, diff_stop, diff_change, etc.\n")
cat("DRSP variables (snake_case): dep, hopeless, anxious, mood_swing, etc.\n")
cat("Craving variables: crave_alc_pm, crave_nic_pm, crave_mar_pm, etc.\n\n")

count_rows_ids(pmdf)

print.variable.names(pmdf)

#vis_miss(pmdf)
```

## Remove Duplicates & Apply Exclusions

```{r manual-exclusions-changes}
# Remove ~55 confirmed duplicate PM surveys (identified by study staff via datepart/timepart)
# Exclude all data from IDs 125, 127 (insufficient data quality)
# Drop temporary date variables (datepart, timepart, b4noon) after removal

#ID 106
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 106 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-08" & pmdf$timepart == "10H 0M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 106 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-13" & pmdf$timepart == "4H 57M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 106 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-19" & pmdf$timepart == "4H 52M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 106 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-22" & pmdf$timepart == "10H 32M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 106 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-23" & pmdf$timepart == "13H 8M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 106 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-24" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 18M 0S"), ]
#ID 107
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 107 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-05-29" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 18M 0S"), ]

#ID 109 
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 109 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-06-22" & pmdf$timepart == "20H 30M 0S"), ]

#ID 113 
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 113 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-06-29" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 25M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 113 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-02" & pmdf$timepart == "23H 03M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 113 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-05" & pmdf$timepart == "0H 21M 0S"), ]
#ID 114
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 114 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-03" & pmdf$timepart == "5H 57M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 114 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-05" & pmdf$timepart == "4H 46M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 114 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-09" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 43M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 114 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-09" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 44M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 114 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-11" & pmdf$timepart == "20H 46M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 114 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-14" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 48M 0S"), ]
#ID 115 
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-06-26" & pmdf$timepart == "8H 20M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-06-28" & pmdf$timepart == "22H 1M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-01" & pmdf$timepart == "19H 52M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-03" & pmdf$timepart == "3H 5M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-09" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 52M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-09" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 54M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-09" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 57M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-10" & pmdf$timepart == "8H 37M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-10" & pmdf$timepart == "8H 40M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "6H 48M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "6H 56M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "6H 59M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 3M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 6M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 8M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 10M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 115 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-07-19" & pmdf$timepart == "7H 12M 0S"), ]

#ID 118 
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 118 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-08" & pmdf$timepart == "12H 26M 0S"), ]
#ID 119 
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-03" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 58M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-08" & pmdf$timepart == "20H 50M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-13" & pmdf$timepart == "5H 37M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-20" & pmdf$timepart == "23H 28M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-22" & pmdf$timepart == "5H 40M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-24" & pmdf$timepart == "16H 23M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 119 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-08-27" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 40M 0S"), ]
#ID 136
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 136 & pmdf$datepart == "2021-11-29" & pmdf$timepart == "17H 10M 0S"), ]
#ID 138
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 138 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-02-06" & pmdf$timepart == "12H 21M 0S"), ]
#ID 143
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 143 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-01-31" & pmdf$timepart == "17H 04M 0S"), ]
#ID 149
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 149 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-05-25" & pmdf$timepart == "0H 42M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 149 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-06-10" & pmdf$timepart == "12H 20M 0S"), ]
#ID 150
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 150 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-06-07" & pmdf$timepart == "21H 34M 0S"), ]
#ID 153
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 153 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-06-23" & pmdf$timepart == "8H 24M 0S"), ]
#ID 159
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 159 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-07-17" & pmdf$timepart == "6H 40M 0S"), ]
#ID 168
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 168 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-09-25" & pmdf$timepart == "20H 23M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 168 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-10-09" & pmdf$timepart == "18H 47M 0S"), ]
#ID 171
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 171 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-10-07" & pmdf$timepart == "0H 10M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 171 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-10-13" & pmdf$timepart == "12H 34M 0S"), ]
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 171 & pmdf$datepart == "2022-10-31" & pmdf$timepart == "20H 36M 0S"), ]
#ID 180
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 180 & pmdf$datepart == "2023-04-15" & pmdf$timepart == "22H 7M 0S"), ]
#ID 181
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 181 & pmdf$datepart == "2023-03-22" & pmdf$timepart == "19H 13M 0S"), ]
#ID 185
pmdf <- pmdf[!(pmdf$id == 185 & pmdf$datepart == "2023-05-29" & pmdf$timepart == "23H 34M 0S"), ]

# ---- Drop id 125 and 127 from both datasets ----
amdf_complete <- amdf_complete %>%
  filter(id != 125, id != 127)
pmdf <- pmdf %>%
  filter(id != 125, id != 127)

##vis_miss(pmdf)

# Check for duplicates and remove
pmdf <- pmdf %>%
  distinct()

# ---- Drop temporary date variables before merge ----
# datepart, timepart, and b4noon were needed for duplicate removal above
# Now drop them before the AM/PM merge to keep only daterated
cat("\n=== Dropping temporary date variables (datepart, timepart, b4noon) before merge ===\n")

# Validation: Check which temporary variables exist before dropping
temp_vars <- c("datepart", "timepart", "b4noon")
existing_temp_vars <- intersect(temp_vars, names(pmdf))
missing_temp_vars <- setdiff(temp_vars, names(pmdf))

if (length(existing_temp_vars) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("Dropping %d temporary variable(s): %s\n", 
              length(existing_temp_vars), 
              paste(existing_temp_vars, collapse = ", ")))
  pmdf <- pmdf %>%
    select(-any_of(existing_temp_vars))
} else {
  cat("No temporary variables to drop (already cleaned)\n")
}

if (length(missing_temp_vars) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("Note: %d expected variable(s) not found: %s\n", 
              length(missing_temp_vars), 
              paste(missing_temp_vars, collapse = ", ")))
}

cat("\nVariables remaining in pmdf:\n")
print(names(pmdf))

count_rows_ids(pmdf)

# Note: Lots of replacements of duplicates etc happen here. 

```

## Visualize Missing Data Pre-Merge

```{r visualize-missingness-pre-am-pm-merge}
# Visualize missingness by AM/PM after cleaning 

# create separate #vis_miss for am and pm 
cat("\n=== AM Survey Missing Data Pattern ===\n")
vis_miss(amdf_complete)
cat("\n=== PM Survey Missing Data Pattern ===\n")
vis_miss(pmdf)

#View(amdf_complete)

```

## Merge AM/PM Surveys

```{r merge_am_pm, warning=FALSE}
# Full join of AM and PM surveys on id + daterated
# Preserves AM-only days (alcohol data), PM-only days (mood data), and complete days
# Validates merge keys, documents missing data patterns

# ---- 1. Pre-Merge Validation ----
cat("\n=== Pre-Merge Validation ===\n")

# Check for duplicate keys in AM data
am_dups <- amdf_complete %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  ungroup()

if(nrow(am_dups) > 0) {
  warning(sprintf("Found %d duplicate id-date combinations in AM data!", nrow(am_dups)))
  print(am_dups %>% select(id, daterated) %>% distinct())
} else {
  cat("✓ No duplicate keys in AM data\n")
}

# Check for duplicate keys in PM data
pm_dups <- pmdf %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  ungroup()

if(nrow(pm_dups) > 0) {
  warning(sprintf("Found %d duplicate id-date combinations in PM data!", nrow(pm_dups)))
  print(pm_dups %>% select(id, daterated) %>% distinct())
} else {
  cat("✓ No duplicate keys in PM data\n")
}

# merge duplicates on id and daterated preferring non-NA values
amdf_complete <- amdf_complete %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(all(is.na(.)), NA, first(na.omit(.)))), .groups = "drop")

#View(amdf_complete)

# ---- 2. Perform Full Join ----
cat("\n=== Performing Full Join ===\n")
cat(sprintf("AM data: %d rows, %d unique IDs\n", nrow(amdf_complete), n_distinct(amdf_complete$id)))
cat(sprintf("PM data: %d rows, %d unique IDs\n", nrow(pmdf), n_distinct(pmdf$id)))

df <- amdf_complete %>%
  full_join(pmdf, by = c("id", "daterated"), suffix = c("_am_dup", "_pm_dup"))

cat(sprintf("\nMerged data: %d rows, %d unique IDs\n", nrow(df), n_distinct(df$id)))

# ---- 3. Analyze Missing Data Patterns ----
cat("\n=== Missing Data Pattern Analysis ===\n")

# Identify data completeness patterns
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    has_am_data = !is.na(num_drinks) | !is.na(crave_alc_am),
    has_pm_data = !is.na(dep) | !is.na(crave_alc_pm),
    data_pattern = case_when(
      has_am_data & has_pm_data ~ "Both_AM_PM",
      has_am_data & !has_pm_data ~ "AM_only",
      !has_am_data & has_pm_data ~ "PM_only",
      TRUE ~ "Neither"
    )
  )

# Summarize patterns
pattern_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(data_pattern) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(id),
    pct_rows = 100 * n() / nrow(df)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_rows))

cat("\nData Completeness Patterns:\n")
print(pattern_summary)

# ---- 4. Visualize Missing Data ----
cat("\n=== Missing Data Visualization ===\n")
#vis_miss(df)

# ---- 5. Identify IDs with Sufficient Data ----
# Create list of IDs with at least 10 days of survey data (any type)
ids_with_survey_data <- df %>%
  filter(data_pattern != "Neither") %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_survey_days = n()) %>%
  filter(n_survey_days >= 10) %>%
  pull(id)

cat(sprintf("\n%d participants have ≥10 days of survey data\n", length(ids_with_survey_data)))

# ---- 6. Final Diagnostics ----
cat("\n=== Merge Complete ===\n")
count_rows_ids(df)


only_in_amdf <- setdiff(names(amdf), names(pmdf))
only_in_pmdf <- setdiff(names(pmdf), names(amdf))

print(list(only_in_amdf = only_in_amdf, only_in_pmdf = only_in_pmdf))

# Final diagnostics: crave_alc_am and crave_alc_pm (AM/PM) min/max and NA counts
crave_cols <- grep("^crave_.*_(am|pm)$", names(df), value = TRUE)
if (length(crave_cols) > 0) {
  crave_summary <- df %>% 
    summarise(across(all_of(crave_cols), 
                     list(N = ~ sum(!is.na(.)), 
                          NA_count = ~ sum(is.na(.)), 
                          min = ~ suppressWarnings(min(., na.rm=TRUE)), 
                          max = ~ suppressWarnings(max(., na.rm=TRUE)))))
  cat("\nFinal crave_* (AM/PM) Summary after merge:\n")
  print(crave_summary)
}


# Create a list of ids with 10 or more days of survey data in this dataset
ids_with_survey_data <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarize(n = n()) %>%
  filter(n >= 10) %>%
  pull(id) %>% unique()

ids_with_survey_data # This is a list that can be used to cross-reference missing data later on

print.variable.names(df)

# sort by id and daterated
df <- df %>%
  arrange(id, daterated)

count_rows_ids(df)

#vis_miss(df)

#remove id 123 observations without any AM survey data
df <- df %>%
  filter(!(id == 123 & is.na(num_drinks)))

#vis_miss(df)

#View(df)

# list number of observations by id
obs_by_id <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_obs = n()) %>%
  arrange(id)

print(obs_by_id)

# Visualize observations by id
p_obs_by_id <- ggplot(obs_by_id, aes(x = factor(id), y = n_obs)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Number of Observations by Participant ID",
       x = "Participant ID",
       y = "Number of Observations") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1))

print(p_obs_by_id)

# save to data prep subfolder
save_png(plot = p_obs_by_id, 
         filename = "obs_by_id_barplot.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder)
```


```{r merge-am-pm-deduplicate}
df <- amdf_complete %>%
  full_join(pmdf, by = c("id", "daterated"), suffix = c("_am_dup", "_pm_dup"))

cat(sprintf("\nMerged data: %d rows, %d unique IDs\n", nrow(df), n_distinct(df$id)))

# ===== ADD THIS CHECKPOINT AND DEDUPLICATION =====
cat("\n=== Checking for duplicates after AM/PM merge ===\n")
df_dupes <- df %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  ungroup()

if (nrow(df_dupes) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("WARNING: Found %d duplicate (id, daterated) rows after AM/PM merge!\n", nrow(df_dupes)))
  cat("Deduplicating by keeping first row per (id, daterated)...\n")
  
  df <- df %>%
    distinct(id, daterated, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  cat(sprintf("Rows after deduplication: %d\n", nrow(df)))
} else {
  cat("✓ No duplicates found\n")
}
# ===== END OF FIX =====

# ---- 3. Analyze Missing Data Patterns ----
cat("\n=== Missing Data Pattern Analysis ===\n")
```


```{r import-merge-visits}
# Merge in study visit dates by id: '/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/02_datasets/UKALC_ALLDATES/03_cleaned_data/Alcohol Session Dates.xlsx'

visit_dates <- read_excel("/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/02_datasets/UKALC_ALLDATES/03_cleaned_data/Alcohol Session Dates.xlsx")

names(visit_dates)

#View(visit_dates)

# ensure id is in the same format
visit_dates <- visit_dates %>%
  mutate(id = as.integer(id))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(id = as.integer(id))
# merge visit dates into df
df <- df %>%
  left_join(visit_dates, by = "id")

# when s1_date or s2_date is equal to daterated, set new variable session_today to 1, else 0
df <- df %>%
  mutate(session_today = case_when(
    daterated == s1_date | daterated == s2_date ~ 1,
    TRUE ~ 0
  ))

# check merge
table(df$session_today, useNA = "ifany")

```







##### TRAIT DATA IMPORT AND MERGE - have to import this here because it's used in next chunk

```{r import-trait-data}

trait_data <- read.csv('/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/02_datasets/UKALC_TRAIT/03_cleaned_data/ukalc_trait.csv')

#View(trait_data)

#str(trait_data)

# set (id) as character, set (age, audit_score, all vars starting with tlfb or pdhq) as number, set (all variables that are 0 or 1) as factors, and set (bev_pref, race, sexorient) as factor, set trait_firststudyperiod as date
trait_data <- trait_data %>%
  mutate(id = as.integer(id),
         age = as.integer(age),
         audit_score = as.integer(audit_score),
         tlfb30d_daysdrank = as.integer(tlfb30d_daysdrank),
         tlfb30d_cumuldrinks = as.numeric(tlfb30d_cumuldrinks),
         tlfb30d_daysbinge = as.integer(tlfb30d_daysbinge),
         tlfb30d_daysdrunk = as.integer(tlfb30d_daysdrunk),
         pdhq_daysdrinkperwk = as.numeric(pdhq_daysdrinkperwk),
         pdhq_numtypicaldrinks = as.numeric(pdhq_numtypicaldrinks),
         pdhq_hrstypdrinking = as.numeric(pdhq_hrstypdrinking),
         med_user = as.factor(med_user),
         sedative_user = as.factor(sedative_user),
         cannabis_user = as.factor(cannabis_user),
         stimulant_user = as.factor(stimulant_user),
         opioid_user = as.factor(opioid_user),
         hallucinogen_user = as.factor(hallucinogen_user),
         pcp_user = as.factor(pcp_user),
         inhalant_user = as.factor(inhalant_user),
         otherdrug_user = as.factor(otherdrug_user),
         bev_pref = as.factor(bev_pref)
         )

# Add AUDIT categories based on audit_score
trait_data <- trait_data %>%
  mutate(audit_category = case_when(
    audit_score >= 0 & audit_score <= 7 ~ "Low Risk",
    audit_score >= 8 & audit_score <= 15 ~ "Hazardous",
    audit_score >= 16 & audit_score <= 19 ~ "Harmful",
    audit_score >= 20 ~ "Possible Dependence",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

# Output appropriate descriptives to the output_folder for all and also print below
trait_summary <- trait_data %>%
  summarise(
    n_participants = n(),
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_age = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_audit_score = mean(audit_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_audit_score = sd(audit_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_weight_kg = mean(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_weight_kg = sd(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_height_cm = mean(height_cm, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_height_cm = sd(height_cm, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("\n=== Trait Data Summary ===\n")
print(trait_summary)

# Merge trait data into df by id
df <- df %>%
  left_join(trait_data, by = "id")

# Print histograms below and save each to output folder

hist_age <- ggplot(trait_data, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution", x = "Age", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()



print(hist_age)

hist_audit <- ggplot(trait_data, aes(x = audit_score)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "AUDIT Score Distribution", x = "AUDIT Score", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

print(hist_audit)

hist_weight <- ggplot(trait_data, aes(x = weight_kg)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Weight (kg) Distribution", x = "Weight (kg)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

print(hist_weight)

hist_height <- ggplot(trait_data, aes(x = height_cm)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Height (cm) Distribution", x = "Height (cm)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

print(hist_height)

# Save histograms to output folder
save_png(plot = hist_age, filename = "trait_age_histogram.png", folder = data_prep_folder)
save_png(plot = hist_audit, filename = "trait_audit_histogram.png", folder = data_prep_folder)
save_png(plot = hist_weight, filename = "trait_weight_histogram.png", folder = data_prep_folder)
save_png(plot = hist_height, filename = "trait_height_histogram.png", folder = data_prep_folder)



```

```{r import-merge-weights}
# =============================================================================
# VALIDATE AND IMPUTE PARTICIPANT WEIGHTS
# =============================================================================
# Validate weight data already present in the dataset and impute missing values
# for BAC calculation.
#
# WHAT IT DOES:
# 1. Checks existing weight_kg column
# 2. Reports statistics in kg
# 3. Imputes missing data with population averages (in kg)
#
# EXPECTED COLUMNS IN DF:
# - id: Participant identifier (numeric)
# - weight_kg: Weight in kilograms (numeric)
#
# OUTPUT: Fills NAs in weight_kg column
# =============================================================================

# Report Initial Stats based on existing data
valid_weights <- df %>% filter(!is.na(weight_kg))

if (nrow(valid_weights) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("Weight range (observed): %.1f - %.1f kg\n",
              min(valid_weights$weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE), 
              max(valid_weights$weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)))
} else {
  warning("No valid weight data found in dataframe before imputation.")
}

# Check for participants without weights
ids_no_weight <- df %>%
  filter(is.na(weight_kg)) %>%
  pull(id) %>%
  unique()

n_missing_before <- length(ids_no_weight)

if (n_missing_before > 0) {
  warning(sprintf("%d participants missing weight data: %s", 
                  n_missing_before, 
                  paste(ids_no_weight, collapse=", ")))
  
  # Use population average weight for females 18-35 when weight is missing
  # Source: CDC/NHANES data for adult women aged 18-39
  POP_AVG_WEIGHT_KG <- 77.1 
  
  # Impute missing values
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      weight_kg = ifelse(is.na(weight_kg), POP_AVG_WEIGHT_KG, weight_kg)
    )
  
  cat(sprintf("\nUsing population average weight (%.1f kg) for %d participants with missing data\n",
              POP_AVG_WEIGHT_KG, n_missing_before))
  cat("Source: CDC/NHANES data for adult women aged 18-39\n")
  
} else {
  cat("\nAll participants have weight data. No imputation needed.\n")
}

```

```{r rename-alcohol-vars}
# =============================================================================
# ALCOHOL VARIABLE CREATION
# =============================================================================
# Creates derived alcohol use variables from the morning survey's report of
# prior evening drinking. These are the PRIMARY OUTCOMES for the study.
#
# NEW VARIABLE NAMES:
# - num_drinks (was Alc_Amt): Number of standard drinks consumed
# - drank_alcohol (was Alc_Drunk): Binary indicator of whether they drank
# - time_start_alcohol (was Alc_Time): Time drinking started
# - dur_alcohol (was Alc_Dur): Duration of drinking episode
#
# WHAT IT DOES:
# 1. Fills missing num_drinks with 0 when participant answered craving question
# 2. Creates lagged versions (prior evening → current day alignment)
# 3. Creates binary indicators for any drinking and binge drinking
#
# KEY CONCEPT - TEMPORAL ALIGNMENT:
# AM surveys ask about PREVIOUS evening (e.g., morning of Jan 2 asks about Jan 1 evening)
# PM surveys ask about CURRENT day (e.g., evening of Jan 1 asks about Jan 1)
# NEED: Align drinking with the day it occurred, not the day it was reported
# SOLUTION: Use lead() to shift num_drinks forward one day
#
# EXAMPLE:
# Row 1: id=101, date=Jan 1, num_drinks=NA (no AM survey yet)
# Row 2: id=101, date=Jan 2, num_drinks=3 (reported drinking 3 drinks on Jan 1)
# After lead(): 
# Row 1: id=101, date=Jan 1, num_drinks_today=3 (drinking happened on Jan 1)
# Row 2: id=101, date=Jan 2, num_drinks_today=NA (will be filled with next day's AM report)
#
# VARIABLES CREATED:
# 1. num_drinks_today: Number of drinks consumed on THIS day (numeric, continuous)
# 2. drink_today: Binary indicator for any drinking (Yes/No factor)
# 3. drink_today_bin: Numeric version of drink_today (0/1)
# 4. fourplustoday: Binary indicator for 4+ drinks (Yes/No factor) 
# 5. fourplustoday_bin: Numeric version of fourplustoday (0/1)
#
# WHY 4+ DRINKS:
# - Standard definition of "binge drinking" for women (NIAAA guidelines)
# - Important threshold for increased risk of negative consequences
# - Primary outcome for examining hormonal effects on heavy drinking
#
# MISSING DATA LOGIC:
# "If crave_alc_am is not missing but num_drinks is missing, set num_drinks to 0"
# RATIONALE: Craving question comes AFTER drinking question in survey
# If participant answered craving, they saw the drinking question
# If num_drinks is missing, they likely skipped it (meaning no drinking)
#
# INPUTS: df (merged AM/PM data)
# OUTPUTS: df with num_drinks_today, drink_today, fourplustoday variables
# =============================================================================

```


```{r align-next-day-alcohol}
library(dplyr)

df <- df %>%
  # Impute 0 for 'num_drinks' if 'crave_alc_am' exists but 'num_drinks' is NA
  mutate(
    num_drinks = if_else(!is.na(crave_alc_am) & is.na(num_drinks), 0, num_drinks)
  ) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  arrange(daterated) %>%
  mutate(
    # Lead variables to align "next day" or "today" values
    # NOTE: lead() shifts values forward by 1 row, so:
    # - "num_drinks" from tomorrow's AM survey → becomes "num_drinks_today"
    # - This aligns drinking reports with the EVENING they occurred
    # - IMPORTANT: The last observation in each ID's time series will be NA
    #   because there's no "next day" to pull from. This is expected behavior.
    num_drinks_today = lead(num_drinks, n = 1),
    dur_alcohol_hours_today = lead(dur_alcohol_hours, n = 1),
    time_start_alcohol_clean_today = lead(time_start_alcohol_clean, n = 1),
    felt_drunk_today = lead(felt_drunk, n = 1),
    
    # --- Create Numeric Binary (0/1) Variables ---
    
    # 1. Any drinking (Threshold > 0)
    drink_today_bin = case_when(
      is.na(num_drinks_today) ~ NA_real_,
      num_drinks_today > 0 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    
    # 2. Binge drinking (Threshold >= 4)
    fourplustoday_bin = case_when(
      is.na(num_drinks_today) ~ NA_real_,
      num_drinks_today >= 4 ~ 1, 
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    
    # 3. Felt Drunk (already 0/1, but cleaned to match drinking data)
    felt_drunk_today_bin = case_when(
      is.na(num_drinks_today) ~ NA_real_,
      num_drinks_today == 0 ~ NA_real_,     # No drinking means felt drunk is NA
      felt_drunk_today == 1 ~ 1,
      felt_drunk_today == 0 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_                  # Keep NA if drinking occurred but feeling is unknown
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  # --- Create Factor Versions (Yes/No) ---
  mutate(
    drink_today = factor(
      drink_today_bin, 
      levels = c(0, 1), 
      labels = c("No", "Yes")
    ),
    fourplustoday = factor(
      fourplustoday_bin, 
      levels = c(0, 1), 
      labels = c("No", "Yes")
    ),
    felt_drunk_today = factor( # Suffix _fac used to distinguish from raw lagged variable
      felt_drunk_today_bin,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("No", "Yes")
    )
  )


# Print narrative of script actions
cat("\n=== Alcohol Variable Creation Summary ===\n")
cat("1. Imputed 0 for 'num_drinks' when 'crave_alc_am' exists but 'num_drinks' is missing.\n")
cat("2. Created lagged variables to align drinking data with the day it occurred.\n")
cat("3. Created binary indicators for any drinking and binge drinking (4+ drinks).\n")
cat("4. Created both numeric (0/1) and factor (Yes/No) versions of drinking indicators.\n")
```


```{r calculate-bac}
# =============================================================================
# BAC (BLOOD ALCOHOL CONCENTRATION) ESTIMATION
# =============================================================================
# Calculate estimated peak BAC using Widmark formula for each drinking episode
#
# ASSUMPTIONS AND LIMITATIONS:
# - All participants are female (r = 0.55 for Widmark constant)
# - Standard drink = 14g ethanol (US definition)
# - Elimination rate = 0.015% per hour (population average)
# - Absorption lag = 0.5 hours after end of drinking
# - Drink size/type unknown (assumes standard drinks)
# - This is an ESTIMATE, not a full pharmacokinetic model
#
# WIDMARK FORMULA:
# BAC = (Alcohol_g / (r × Weight_g)) × 100
# Where: r = 0.55 for females, Weight_g = weight in grams
#
# PEAK BAC TIMING:
# Peak occurs at: start_time + duration + 0.5 hours (absorption lag)
# By peak time, some alcohol has been eliminated: 0.015% × hours_elapsed
#
# INPUTS:
# - num_drinks: Number of standard drinks consumed
# - dur_hours: Duration of drinking episode in hours
# - weight_kg: Participant weight in kilograms (uses pop avg 77.1kg if missing)
# - start_time: Time drinking started (HH:MM format, optional)
#
# OUTPUTS:
# - alcohol_g: Total alcohol consumed in grams
# - bac_peak_percent: Estimated peak BAC in percent (e.g., 0.08 for 0.08%)
# - bac_peak_mgdl: Estimated peak BAC in mg/dL
# - bac_peak_time: Estimated time of peak BAC (if start_time provided)
#
# NOTE: Population average weight (170 lbs / 77.1 kg) is automatically used
# when individual weight data is missing. Source: CDC/NHANES for women 18-39.
# =============================================================================

#' Calculate Peak Blood Alcohol Concentration (BAC)
#'
#' Estimates peak BAC using the Widmark formula with elimination adjustment.
#' Designed for female participants consuming alcohol over a specified duration.
#'
#' @param num_drinks Numeric. Number of standard drinks consumed (14g ethanol each)
#' @param dur_hours Numeric. Duration of drinking episode in hours
#' @param weight_kg Numeric. Body weight in kilograms
#' @param start_time Character. Time drinking started in HH:MM format (24-hour)
#' @param date Date. Date of drinking episode (for calculating peak time)
#'
#' @return List with elements:
#'   - alcohol_g: Total alcohol in grams
#'   - bac_peak_percent: Peak BAC in percent
#'   - bac_peak_mgdl: Peak BAC in mg/dL
#'   - bac_peak_time: POSIXct datetime of peak (if start_time and date provided)
#'
#' @details
#' Uses Widmark constant r=0.55 for females, elimination rate of 0.015%/hr,
#' and assumes peak occurs 0.5 hours after drinking ends (absorption lag).
#' Note: The pipeline uses population average weight (77.1 kg) when individual
#' weight data is missing, based on CDC/NHANES data for women aged 18-39.
#'
#' @examples
#' calc_peak_bac(3, 2, 64)   # 3 drinks over 2 hours, 64 kg
#' calc_peak_bac(0, 0, 64)   # No drinking
#' calc_peak_bac(3, 2, 77.1) # Using population average weight
calc_peak_bac <- function(num_drinks, dur_hours, weight_kg, start_time = NA, date = NA) {
  
  # Initialize output list
  result <- list(
    alcohol_g = NA_real_,
    bac_peak_percent = NA_real_,
    bac_peak_mgdl = NA_real_,
    bac_peak_time = as.POSIXct(NA)
  )
  
  # Handle edge case: no drinks or zero drinks
  if (is.na(num_drinks) || num_drinks == 0) {
    result$alcohol_g <- 0
    result$bac_peak_percent <- 0
    result$bac_peak_mgdl <- 0
    return(result)
  }
  
  # Handle edge case: missing weight
  if (is.na(weight_kg)) {
    result$alcohol_g <- num_drinks * 14
    # BAC values remain NA (cannot calculate without weight)
    return(result)
  }
  
  # Constants
  GRAMS_PER_DRINK <- 14        # US standard drink = 14g ethanol
  WIDMARK_R_FEMALE <- 0.55     # Body water constant for females
  ELIMINATION_RATE <- 0.015    # BAC elimination: 0.015% per hour
  ABSORPTION_LAG <- 0.5        # Hours after drinking ends until peak
  
  # Calculate total alcohol consumed (grams)
  alcohol_g <- num_drinks * GRAMS_PER_DRINK
  
  # Handle edge case: missing or zero duration
  if (is.na(dur_hours)) {
    dur_hours <- 0  # Assume instantaneous consumption if duration unknown
  }
  
  # Convert weight to grams
  weight_g <- weight_kg * 1000
  
  # Calculate raw BAC at end of drinking (before elimination)
  # Widmark formula: BAC = (alcohol_g / (r × weight_g)) × 100
  bac_raw <- (alcohol_g / (WIDMARK_R_FEMALE * weight_g)) * 100
  
  # Time from start of drinking to peak (duration + absorption lag)
  t_peak <- dur_hours + ABSORPTION_LAG
  
  # Apply elimination: subtract BAC eliminated by peak time
  bac_peak_percent <- bac_raw - (ELIMINATION_RATE * t_peak)
  
  # Clamp to non-negative (can't have negative BAC)
  bac_peak_percent <- max(bac_peak_percent, 0)
  
  # Convert to mg/dL
  bac_peak_mgdl <- bac_peak_percent * 1000
  
  # Calculate peak time if start time and date are provided
  bac_peak_time <- as.POSIXct(NA)
  if (!is.na(start_time) && !is.na(date)) {
    tryCatch({
      # Parse start time (HH:MM format)
      time_parts <- as.numeric(strsplit(start_time, ":")[[1]])
      if (length(time_parts) == 2) {
        # Combine date and time
        start_datetime <- as.POSIXct(paste(date, start_time), 
                                      format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M",
                                      tz = "America/New_York")
        # Add duration and absorption lag
        bac_peak_time <- start_datetime + (t_peak * 3600)  # Convert hours to seconds
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      # If time parsing fails, leave as NA
    })
  }
  
  # Return results
  result$alcohol_g <- alcohol_g
  result$bac_peak_percent <- bac_peak_percent
  result$bac_peak_mgdl <- bac_peak_mgdl
  result$bac_peak_time <- bac_peak_time
  
  return(result)
}

# Apply BAC calculation to dataset
cat("\n=== Calculating Peak BAC Estimates ===\n")

# Check if weight data is available
has_weights <- "weight_kg" %in% names(df) && sum(!is.na(df$weight_kg)) > 0

if (has_weights) {
  
  # Calculate BAC for each row
  # Use pmap to pass multiple columns to calc_peak_bac function
  bac_results <- df %>%
    select(num_drinks_today, dur_alcohol_hours_today, weight_kg, 
           time_start_alcohol_clean_today, daterated) %>%
    pmap(function(num_drinks_today, dur_alcohol_hours_today, weight_kg, 
                  time_start_alcohol_clean_today, daterated) {
      calc_peak_bac(
        num_drinks = num_drinks_today,
        dur_hours = dur_alcohol_hours_today,
        weight_kg = weight_kg,
        start_time = time_start_alcohol_clean_today,
        date = daterated
      )
    })
  
  # Extract results into separate columns
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      alcohol_g = map_dbl(bac_results, ~ .x$alcohol_g),
      bac_peak_percent = map_dbl(bac_results, ~ .x$bac_peak_percent),
      bac_peak_mgdl = map_dbl(bac_results, ~ .x$bac_peak_mgdl),
      bac_peak_time = map(bac_results, ~ .x$bac_peak_time) %>% 
        map(as.POSIXct, origin = "1970-01-01") %>%
        do.call(c, .)
    )
  
  # Report summary statistics
  n_drinking_days <- sum(df$num_drinks_today > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_bac_calculated <- sum(!is.na(df$bac_peak_percent) & df$bac_peak_percent > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  cat(sprintf("Drinking days: %d\n", n_drinking_days))
  cat(sprintf("BAC calculated: %d (%.1f%% of drinking days)\n", 
              n_bac_calculated, 100 * n_bac_calculated / max(n_drinking_days, 1)))
  
  if (n_bac_calculated > 0) {
    bac_summary <- df %>%
      filter(!is.na(bac_peak_percent) & bac_peak_percent > 0) %>%
      summarise(
        mean_bac = mean(bac_peak_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_bac = median(bac_peak_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
        min_bac = min(bac_peak_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
        max_bac = max(bac_peak_percent, na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    
    cat(sprintf("\nBAC Summary (drinking days only):\n"))
    cat(sprintf("  Mean: %.3f%% (%.0f mg/dL)\n", 
                bac_summary$mean_bac, bac_summary$mean_bac * 1000))
    cat(sprintf("  Median: %.3f%% (%.0f mg/dL)\n", 
                bac_summary$median_bac, bac_summary$median_bac * 1000))
    cat(sprintf("  Range: %.3f%% - %.3f%%\n", 
                bac_summary$min_bac, bac_summary$max_bac))
    
    # Report binge drinking threshold (0.08% BAC)
    n_binge_bac <- sum(df$bac_peak_percent >= 0.08, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat(sprintf("  Days with BAC ≥ 0.08%%: %d (%.1f%% of drinking days)\n",
                n_binge_bac, 100 * n_binge_bac / max(n_drinking_days, 1)))
  }
  
  cat("\nNOTE: BAC estimates assume:\n")
  cat("  - Female participants (r=0.55)\n")
  cat("  - Standard drinks (14g ethanol each)\n")
  cat("  - Elimination rate: 0.015%/hour\n")
  cat("  - Peak at drinking_end + 0.5 hours\n")
  cat("  - Population average weight (77.1 kg) used when individual weight missing\n")
  
} else {
  warning("Weight data not available. BAC calculations skipped.")
  cat("\nTo calculate BAC, ensure weights.csv is in the data directory with columns: id, weight\n")
  
  # Add placeholder BAC columns
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      alcohol_g = ifelse(!is.na(num_drinks_today), num_drinks_today * 14, NA_real_),
      bac_peak_percent = NA_real_,
      bac_peak_mgdl = NA_real_,
      bac_peak_time = as.POSIXct(NA)
    )
}


# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VALIDATE BINARY VARIABLES
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Binary variables (drink_today_bin, fourplustoday_bin, felt_drunk_today_bin)
# were correctly created in the create-alc-vars chunk above.
# This section validates that they contain only 0, 1, or NA values.

# Validate binary variables are correctly coded (0/1 only)
cat("\n=== Binary Variable Validation ===\n")
for (var in c("drink_today_bin", "fourplustoday_bin", "felt_drunk_today_bin")) {
  unique_vals <- unique(df[[var]])
  unique_vals <- unique_vals[!is.na(unique_vals)]
  if (!all(unique_vals %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop(sprintf("ERROR: %s contains non-binary values: %s", 
                 var, paste(unique_vals, collapse=", ")))
  }
  cat(sprintf("✓ %s: Valid binary (0/1) with %d values\n", 
              var, sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))))
}

# Display structure and distribution
str(df$drink_today_bin)
hist(df$drink_today_bin, main = "Distribution of drink_today_bin", xlab = "Value")
str(df$fourplustoday_bin)
hist(df$fourplustoday_bin, main = "Distribution of fourplustoday_bin", xlab = "Value")

#vis_miss(df)
```

```{r score-bdefs}
# =============================================================================
# BDEFS COMPOSITE SCORE CALCULATION (FOR UKALC DIAGNOSTIC PLOTS)
# =============================================================================
# NOTE: These subscales are calculated here for UKALC diagnostic histograms,
# but they are ALSO calculated in UKALC_03_processing_shared.Rmd for the 
# merged ADHD+UKALC dataset to ensure both studies receive identical processing.
#
# Creates a single composite score from Barkley's Deficits in Executive 
# Functioning Scale (BDEFS). This measures executive function and impulse control.
#
# WHAT IT DOES:
# - Calculates row-wise mean of 7 bdefs items
# - Handles missing data by using na.rm=TRUE (calculates mean of available items)
# - Creates histogram of distribution
#
# bdefs ITEMS (defined in bdefs_vars list):
# 1. diff_inhib: Difficulty inhibiting responses
# 2. diff_stop: Difficulty stopping ongoing behavior
# 3. diff_change: Difficulty changing course
# 4. immed_payoff: Preference for immediate payoff
# 5. res_urge: Difficulty resisting urges
# 6. diff_long_rew: Difficulty waiting for long-term rewards
# 7. diff_res_rew: Difficulty resisting rewarding stimuli
#
# SCALE PROPERTIES:
# - Range: 1-4 (Never/Rarely=1, Sometimes=2, Often=3, Very Often=4)
# - Higher scores = Greater deficits in executive functioning (worse impulse control)
# - Composite range: 1-4 (mean of item scores)
#
# WHY THIS MATTERS:
# - Executive dysfunction predicts alcohol use and problems
# - May vary across menstrual cycle phases
# - Key mediator in hormonal effects on drinking
#
# MISSING DATA HANDLING:
# - na.rm=TRUE means: Calculate mean of available items
# - If participant has 5/7 items, mean is based on those 5
# - If ALL items missing, result is NaN (not a number)
# - Decision: Include participants with partial data (not dropping if <7 items)
# - Assumption: Missing items are missing at random (MAR)
#
# INPUTS: df with bdefs items (from PM surveys)
# OUTPUTS: df with bdefs composite score + histogram PNG
# =============================================================================

# Variables needed for THIS chunk only
bdefs_vars <- c(
  "diff_inhib", "diff_stop", "diff_change", "immed_payoff", 
  "res_urge", "diff_long_rew", "diff_res_rew"
)

# Create a mean score for these daily items across each row and make sure to account for missing values (diff_inhib, diff_stop, diff_change, immed_payoff, res_urge, diff_long_rew, diff_res_rew)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(bdefs_total = rowMeans(select(., all_of(bdefs_vars)), na.rm = TRUE))

# Create and save histogram for bdefs_total using ggplot2
p_bdefs_total <- ggplot(df, aes(x = bdefs_total)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "lightblue", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "BDEFS Total Distribution", x = "BDEFS Total", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
save_png(plot = p_bdefs_total, 
         filename = "bdefs_total_histogram.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder)

# score the mean of bdefs_impulsiveaction
df <- df %>%
  mutate(bdefs_impulsiveaction = rowMeans(select(., diff_inhib, diff_stop, diff_change), na.rm = TRUE))

# Create and save histogram for bdefs_impulsiveaction
p_bdefs_impulsiveaction <- ggplot(df, aes(x = bdefs_impulsiveaction)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "lightblue", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "BDEFS Impulsive Action Distribution", x = "BDEFS Impulsive Action", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
save_png(plot = p_bdefs_impulsiveaction, 
         filename = "bdefs_impulsiveaction_histogram.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder)

# score the mean of bdefs_delayaversion
df <- df %>%
  mutate(bdefs_delayaversion = rowMeans(select(., immed_payoff, res_urge, diff_long_rew, diff_res_rew), na.rm = TRUE))

# Create and save histogram for bdefs_delayaversion
p_bdefs_delayaversion <- ggplot(df, aes(x = bdefs_delayaversion)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "lightblue", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "BDEFS Delay Aversion Distribution", x = "BDEFS Delay Aversion", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
save_png(plot = p_bdefs_delayaversion, 
         filename = "bdefs_delayaversion_histogram.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder)

```





```{r cycle-dates-import-clean}
# =============================================================================
# MENSTRUAL CYCLE DATA IMPORT & CLEANING
# =============================================================================
# Imports comprehensive participant tracking data including RTB-recoded menses 
# dates, multiple ovulation dates, and exclusion flags. This is essential for 
# menstrual cycle phase analysis.
#
# WHAT IT DOES:
# 1. Loads UKALC_Participant_Data_Tracker with all cycle dates and metadata
# 2. Creates TWO versions of menses dates:
#    - period1/2/3 (non-RTB): Original MP dates only
#    - period1/2/3_rtb (RTB): RTB-recoded dates, falls back to MP if no RTB
# 3. Creates event flags for both versions (menses/ovtoday and menses_rtb/ovtoday_rtb)
# 4. Applies exclusion flags (Excl_all_id, Excl_CycData, Excl_Horm)
# 5. Calculates cycle length for both versions
# 6. Later: Runs menstrualcycleR for both versions (see run-menstrualcycler chunk)
#
# DATA SOURCE:
# File: UKALC_Participant_Data_Tracker_20251225.csv
# Format: One row per participant with comprehensive cycle and metadata
# Variables:
#   - id: Participant ID
#   - MP1, MP2, MP3: Original menses onset dates (1st, 2nd, 3rd)
#   - RTBMP1, RTBMP2, RTBMP3: RTB-recoded menses dates (RTB = Return To Baseline of progesterone)
#   - EDO1, EDO2, EDO3: Estimated day of ovulation dates (1st, 2nd, 3rd)
#   - Ov_Status: Ovulatory status (categorical)
#   - Assay_Batch: Which assay batch participant was in
#   - Excl_all_id: Exclude from ALL datasets (logical)
#   - Excl_Horm: Exclude from hormone analyses (logical)
#   - Excl_CycData: Exclude cycle data but keep survey data (logical)
#   - Assay_Plate: List of assay plates for participant
#
# ASSAY PLATE RECODING:
# - Raw Assay_Plate is comma-separated list (e.g., "1,2,3" or "3")
# - Created variables for analysis:
#   * n_assay_plates: Count of plates participant was on (0 if NA)
#   * in_plate_X: Binary indicators for each plate (dynamically created based on actual plate numbers)
#   * Assay_Plate_orig: Original string preserved
# - These allow testing for plate effects and batch corrections
#
# WHY MENSTRUAL CYCLE MATTERS:
# - Hormones (e2, p4) fluctuate dramatically across cycle
# - Different phases have different hormone profiles:
#   * Follicular: Low p4, rising e2
#   * Ovulatory: e2 peak, lh surge
#   * Luteal: High p4, moderate e2
#   * Menstrual: Low e2 and p4
# - Alcohol responses may vary by phase
#
# RTB RECODING - DUAL VERSION APPROACH:
# RTB = "Return To Baseline" - period dates recoded based on progesterone return to baseline
# Creates TWO parallel versions of all cycle-related variables:
# 1. NON-RTB VERSION (original variable names): Uses only original MP dates
#    - period1, period2, period3 = MP1, MP2, MP3
#    - menses, ovtoday = event flags from MP dates
#    - cycle_length = period2 - period1
#    - menstrualcycleR outputs: cyclic_time_impute, cyclic_time_fwd/bwd, etc.
# 2. RTB VERSION (_rtb suffix): Uses RTB-recoded dates when available, falls back to MP
#    - period1_rtb, period2_rtb, period3_rtb = coalesce(RTBMP*, MP*)
#    - menses_rtb, ovtoday_rtb = event flags from RTB dates (or MP if no RTB)
#    - cycle_length_rtb = period2_rtb - period1_rtb
```
