```{r cycle-dates-import-clean}
#    - menstrualcycleR outputs: cyclic_time_impute_rtb, cyclic_time_fwd/bwd_rtb, etc.
# This allows analyses comparing RTB-recoded vs original dates
# Track which dates were recoded with period*_recoded boolean flags
#
# EXCLUSION FLAGS:
# - Excl_all_id = TRUE: Remove ALL data for this participant
# - Excl_CycData = TRUE: Keep survey data, but set cycle variables to NA
# - Excl_Horm = TRUE: Flag for later exclusion from hormone analyses
#
# CYCLE LENGTH CALCULATION:
# - cycle_length = period2 - period1 (in days)
# - Valid range: Typically 21-38 days (biological normal)
# - Participants outside this range are filtered from cycle data
# - BUT: They are NOT removed from survey data
#
# IMPORTANT DESIGN DECISION:
# OLD approach: Filtered survey data to only include participants with valid cycles
# NEW approach: Keep ALL survey data, even if cycle data is invalid
# Rationale: Survey data is still valuable for non-cycle analyses
# Impact: Some participants will have NA for cycle-related variables
#
# EVENT FLAGS:
# - menses: Binary indicator (1=period onset day, 0=other) - includes ALL menses dates
# - ovtoday: Binary indicator (1=ovulation day, 0=other) - includes ALL ovulation dates
# - Used for: Plotting cycle events, defining cycle phases
# - Merged to survey data by id and daterated
#
# CYCLE PHASE VISUALIZATION:
# - Creates plots showing period and ovulation events across study
# - Red vertical lines = period onset
# - Green vertical lines = ovulation
# - Helps identify data quality issues (irregular cycles, missing events)
#
# INPUTS: 
#   - UKALC_Participant_Data_Tracker_20251225.csv
#   - Survey data (df, amdf, pmdf) for date imputation
# OUTPUTS:
#   - df with menses and ovtoday columns (replacing TAEMPeriodStart and TAEMOvPos)
#   - cycle_length variable
#   - Participant metadata (Ov_Status, Assay_Batch, exclusion flags)
#   - Diagnostic output and plots
# =============================================================================

# ---- 1. Import the comprehensive participant tracking data ----
raw_cycle_df <- read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/02_datasets/UKALC_ALLDATES/03_cleaned_data/UKALC_Participant_Data_Tracker_20251231.csv")

cat("\n=== Imported UKALC Participant Data Tracker ===\n")


# select only relevant columns
raw_cycle_df <- raw_cycle_df %>%
  select(id, MP1, MP2, MP3, RTBMP1, RTBMP2, RTBMP3, EDO1, EDO2,
         Ov_Status, Assay_Batch, Assay_Plate,
         Excl_all_id, Excl_Horm, Excl_CycData,
         e_spike_date_remove, e_spike_date_remove_2, p_spike_date_remove, p_spike_date_remove_2)

#View(raw_cycle_df)

# Parse dates and create "best" menses dates prioritizing RTB-recoded dates
wide_cycles <- raw_cycle_df %>%
  mutate(
    # Ensure id is character
    id = as.character(id),
    
    # Parse all date columns (in M/D/YY format)
    MP1 = mdy(MP1),
    MP2 = mdy(MP2),
    MP3 = mdy(MP3),
    RTBMP1 = mdy(RTBMP1),
    RTBMP2 = mdy(RTBMP2),
    RTBMP3 = mdy(RTBMP3),
    EDO1 = mdy(EDO1),
    EDO2 = mdy(EDO2),
    espike1 = mdy(e_spike_date_remove),
    espike2 = mdy(e_spike_date_remove_2),
    pspike1 = mdy(p_spike_date_remove),
    pspike2 = mdy(p_spike_date_remove_2),
    
    # Convert exclusion flags to logical
    Excl_all_id = as.logical(Excl_all_id),
    Excl_Horm = as.logical(Excl_Horm),
    Excl_CycData = as.logical(Excl_CycData),
    
    # Create RTB-prioritized menses dates (with _rtb suffix)
    period1_rtb = coalesce(RTBMP1, MP1),
    period2_rtb = coalesce(RTBMP2, MP2),
    period3_rtb = coalesce(RTBMP3, MP3),
    
    # Create non-RTB menses dates (original MP dates only)
    period1 = MP1,
    period2 = MP2,
    period3 = MP3,
    
    # Track which dates were recoded
    period1_recoded = !is.na(RTBMP1),
    period2_recoded = !is.na(RTBMP2),
    period3_recoded = !is.na(RTBMP3)
  ) %>%
  # Select relevant columns
  select(id, period1, period2, period3, 
         period1_rtb, period2_rtb, period3_rtb,
         period1_recoded, period2_recoded, period3_recoded,
         EDO1, EDO2, Ov_Status, Assay_Batch, Assay_Plate,
         Excl_all_id, Excl_Horm, Excl_CycData, espike1, espike2, pspike1, pspike2)

#View(wide_cycles)


# for ids where Excl_CycData is TRUE, set all period* and EDO* dates to NA
wide_cycles <- wide_cycles %>%
  mutate(
    period1 = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), period1),
    period2 = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), period2),
    period3 = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), period3),
    period1_rtb = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), period1_rtb),
    period2_rtb = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), period2_rtb),
    period3_rtb = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), period3_rtb),
    EDO1 = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), EDO1),
    EDO2 = if_else(Excl_CycData %in% TRUE, as.Date(NA), EDO2)
  )





# ---- Parse Assay_Plate for analysis ----
# Assay_Plate can be comma-separated list (e.g., "1,2,3" or "3")
# Create indicator variables and count variable
wide_cycles <- wide_cycles %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert Assay_Plate to character and handle NA
    Assay_Plate_orig = as.character(Assay_Plate),
    
    # Count number of plates (count commas + 1, or 0 if NA)
    n_assay_plates = if_else(
      is.na(Assay_Plate_orig), 
      0L, 
      as.integer(str_count(Assay_Plate_orig, ",") + 1)
    )
  )

# ---- Parse Assay Plate Indicators Dynamically ----
# Extract all unique plate numbers from the Assay_Plate column
all_plates <- wide_cycles %>%
  filter(!is.na(Assay_Plate_orig)) %>%
  pull(Assay_Plate_orig) %>%
  str_split(",") %>%
  unlist() %>%
  str_trim() %>%
  unique() %>%
  as.numeric() %>%
  sort()

# Create binary indicator for each plate dynamically
for (plate_num in all_plates) {
  col_name <- paste0("in_plate_", plate_num)
  wide_cycles <- wide_cycles %>%
    mutate(!!col_name := str_detect(Assay_Plate_orig, paste0("\\b", plate_num, "\\b")) & !is.na(Assay_Plate_orig))
}

# ---- Diagnostic Output: RTB Recoding Statistics ----
cat("\n=== RTB RECODING STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Period 1 - Recoded:", sum(wide_cycles$period1_recoded, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "/ Original:", sum(!wide_cycles$period1_recoded & !is.na(wide_cycles$period1), na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Period 2 - Recoded:", sum(wide_cycles$period2_recoded, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "/ Original:", sum(!wide_cycles$period2_recoded & !is.na(wide_cycles$period2), na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Period 3 - Recoded:", sum(wide_cycles$period3_recoded, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "/ Original:", sum(!wide_cycles$period3_recoded & !is.na(wide_cycles$period3), na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

cat("\n=== OVULATION DATE STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Participants with EDO1:", sum(!is.na(wide_cycles$EDO1)), "\n")
cat("Participants with EDO2:", sum(!is.na(wide_cycles$EDO2)), "\n")

cat("\n=== EXCLUSION FLAG STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Excl_all_id (exclude from all datasets):", sum(wide_cycles$Excl_all_id, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Excl_CycData (exclude cycle data only):", sum(wide_cycles$Excl_CycData, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Excl_Horm (exclude from hormone analyses):", sum(wide_cycles$Excl_Horm, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

cat("\n=== ASSAY PLATE STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Participants by number of plates:\n")
print(table(wide_cycles$n_assay_plates, useNA = "ifany"))
cat("\nPlate numbers found:", paste(all_plates, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Participants in each plate:\n")
for (plate_num in all_plates) {
  col_name <- paste0("in_plate_", plate_num)
  cat(sprintf("Plate %s: %d\n", plate_num, sum(wide_cycles[[col_name]], na.rm = TRUE)))
}

# ---- 2. Calculate Cycle Length (No Imputation) ----
# Only use dates from the tracker - do NOT impute missing dates
# Calculate for both RTB and non-RTB versions
wide_cycles_with_length <- wide_cycles %>%
  filter(!Excl_CycData | is.na(Excl_CycData)) %>%  # Keep only non-excluded participants
  mutate(
    cycle_length = as.numeric(period2 - period1),
    cycle_length_rtb = as.numeric(period2_rtb - period1_rtb)
  ) %>%
  # Filter to ensure at least ONE version has valid cycle data
  # Design choice: Allow participants with EITHER version (RTB or non-RTB) to be included
  # This means some participants may have NA for one version but valid data for the other
  # If both versions are required, change to: filter(!is.na(cycle_length) & !is.na(cycle_length_rtb))
  filter(!is.na(cycle_length) | !is.na(cycle_length_rtb))

# ---- 3. Apply Exclusion Flags ----
if (!exists("df")) stop("df (survey data) must be loaded first.")

# First, remove participants with Excl_all_id = TRUE from main dataset
ids_excl_all <- wide_cycles %>% 
  filter(Excl_all_id == TRUE) %>% 
  pull(id)

cat("\n=== APPLYING EXCLUSIONS TO MAIN DATASET ===\n")
cat("Removing", length(ids_excl_all), "participants with Excl_all_id = TRUE:", 
    paste(ids_excl_all, collapse = ", "), "\n")

clean_df <- df %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id), daterated = as_date(daterated)) %>%
  filter(!id %in% ids_excl_all) %>%  # Remove Excl_all_id participants
  filter(!is.na(daterated))


# print what's been done
cat("Main dataset now has", nrow(clean_df), "observations after applying Excl_all_id exclusions.\n")
cat("Participants with valid cycle data (non-RTB or RTB) retained for cycle analyses:", 
    n_distinct(wide_cycles_with_length$id), "\n")

# ---- 4. Create Event Flags for Menses and Ovulation ----
# Create TWO versions: RTB-prioritized (_rtb suffix) and non-RTB (original names)

# RTB VERSION: Uses period*_rtb dates (RTB dates with MP fallback)
events_long_rtb <- bind_rows(
  # Menses: Include ALL three period dates (RTB version)
  wide_cycles_with_length %>% 
    select(id, period1_rtb, period2_rtb, period3_rtb) %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = c(period1_rtb, period2_rtb, period3_rtb), 
                 values_to = "daterated", 
                 names_to = "period_num") %>% 
    filter(!is.na(daterated)) %>%
    mutate(menses_rtb = 1) %>% 
    select(id, daterated, menses_rtb),
  
  # Ovulation: Include BOTH ovulation dates (same for both versions)
  wide_cycles_with_length %>% 
    select(id, EDO1, EDO2) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(EDO1, EDO2), 
                 values_to = "daterated", 
                 names_to = "ov_num") %>% 
    filter(!is.na(daterated)) %>%
    mutate(ovtoday_rtb = 1) %>%
    select(id, daterated, ovtoday_rtb)
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(daterated)) %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  summarize(
    menses_rtb = max(menses_rtb, na.rm = TRUE),
    ovtoday_rtb = max(ovtoday_rtb, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    menses_rtb = if_else(is.finite(menses_rtb), menses_rtb, NA_real_),
    ovtoday_rtb = if_else(is.finite(ovtoday_rtb), ovtoday_rtb, NA_real_)
  )

# NON-RTB VERSION: Uses original period* dates (MP only, no RTB)
events_long <- bind_rows(
  # Menses: Include ALL three period dates (non-RTB version)
  wide_cycles_with_length %>% 
    select(id, period1, period2, period3) %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = c(period1, period2, period3), 
                 values_to = "daterated", 
                 names_to = "period_num") %>% 
    filter(!is.na(daterated)) %>%
    mutate(menses = 1) %>% 
    select(id, daterated, menses),
  
  # Ovulation: Include BOTH ovulation dates (same for both versions)
  wide_cycles_with_length %>% 
    select(id, EDO1, EDO2) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(EDO1, EDO2), 
                 values_to = "daterated", 
                 names_to = "ov_num") %>% 
    filter(!is.na(daterated)) %>%
    mutate(ovtoday = 1) %>%
    select(id, daterated, ovtoday)
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(daterated)) %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  summarize(
    menses = max(menses, na.rm = TRUE),
    ovtoday = max(ovtoday, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    menses = if_else(is.finite(menses), menses, NA_real_),
    ovtoday = if_else(is.finite(ovtoday), ovtoday, NA_real_)
  )

clean_df <- clean_df %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id))

# Also ensure wide_cycles has character id (already done on line 1361, but double-check)
wide_cycles <- wide_cycles %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id))


# CHECKPOINT A: Right BEFORE the cycle data merge (before line 1605)
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT A: Before Cycle Merge ===\n")
clean_df_dupes <- clean_df %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  summarise(n_rows = n(), .groups = "drop")
cat("Duplicate (id, daterated) in clean_df:", nrow(clean_df_dupes), "\n")

#View(clean_df)

# ---- 5. Merge Event Flags and Metadata into Main Dataframe ----
final_df <- clean_df %>%
  # Merge NON-RTB cycle events
  full_join(events_long, by = c("id", "daterated")) %>%
  # Merge RTB cycle events
  full_join(events_long_rtb, by = c("id", "daterated")) %>%
  # Merge cycle lengths from both versions
  left_join(wide_cycles_with_length %>%
              select(id, cycle_length, cycle_length_rtb),
            by = "id") %>%
  # Merge participant metadata from ALL participants (not just those with valid cycles)
  left_join(
    wide_cycles %>%
      select(
        id,
        Ov_Status,
        Assay_Batch,
        Assay_Plate,
        Assay_Plate_orig,
        espike1,
        espike2,
        pspike1,
        pspike2,
        n_assay_plates,
        starts_with("in_plate_"),
        Excl_Horm,
        Excl_CycData
      ),
    by = "id"
    ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Fill NAs for event flags with 0 (missing = no event on that day)
    menses = replace_na(menses, 0),
    ovtoday = replace_na(ovtoday, 0),
    menses_rtb = replace_na(menses_rtb, 0),
    ovtoday_rtb = replace_na(ovtoday_rtb, 0),
    id = as.character(id),
  ) %>%
  arrange(id, daterated)


# ADD CHECKPOINT RIGHT HERE (after line ~1650):
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT:  After All Cycle Merges ===\n")
final_df_dupes <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  summarise(n_rows = n(), .groups = "drop")
cat("Duplicate (id, daterated) in final_df:", nrow(final_df_dupes), "\n")

if (nrow(final_df_dupes) > 0) {
  cat("WARNING: Found duplicate (id, daterated) combinations!\n")
  cat("Showing first 20 duplicates:\n")
  print(head(final_df_dupes, 20))
  
  # Show example of what's duplicated for ID 118
  cat("\n=== Example:  ID 118 duplicates ===\n")
  final_df %>%
    filter(id == "118") %>%
    group_by(daterated) %>%
    filter(n() > 1) %>%
    select(id, daterated, Excl_Horm, e2, p4, lh, menses, ovtoday) %>%
    arrange(daterated) %>%
    print(n = 20)
}



# ---- 7. Final Output and Diagnostics ----
cat("\n=== FINAL DATASET SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Final Rows:", nrow(final_df), "\n")
cat("Unique IDs:", n_distinct(final_df$id), "\n")

cat("\n=== NON-RTB VERSION (Original MP dates) ===\n")
cat("IDs with menses events:", n_distinct(final_df$id[final_df$menses == 1]), "\n")
cat("IDs with ovulation events:", n_distinct(final_df$id[final_df$ovtoday == 1]), "\n")
cat("Total menses events:", sum(final_df$menses, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Total ovulation events:", sum(final_df$ovtoday, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

cat("\n=== RTB VERSION (RTB-prioritized dates) ===\n")
cat("IDs with menses_rtb events:", n_distinct(final_df$id[final_df$menses_rtb == 1]), "\n")
cat("IDs with ovulation_rtb events:", n_distinct(final_df$id[final_df$ovtoday_rtb == 1]), "\n")
cat("Total menses_rtb events:", sum(final_df$menses_rtb, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Total ovulation_rtb events:", sum(final_df$ovtoday_rtb, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

count_rows_ids(final_df)

# ---- 8. Track Excluded IDs for Documentation ----
# Track IDs that were excluded due to various reasons for later reference
# Note: ids_excl_all was already defined earlier when applying exclusions

ids_excl_cycdata <- wide_cycles %>% 
  filter(Excl_CycData == TRUE) %>% 
  pull(id)

# Manual exclusions (hardcoded removals)
ids_manual_excl <- c() # Add any manual exclusions here if needed

# Combined list of IDs with no menses data (excluded or missing cycle data)
ids_w_no_menses_onset <- unique(c(ids_excl_all, ids_excl_cycdata, ids_manual_excl))

cat("\n=== IDs EXCLUDED FROM CYCLE DATA ===\n")
cat("IDs with Excl_all_id:", paste(ids_excl_all, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("IDs with Excl_CycData:", paste(ids_excl_cycdata, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("IDs with manual exclusions:", paste(ids_manual_excl, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("All IDs without menses data:", paste(ids_w_no_menses_onset, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Assign final_df back to df for consistency with later code
df <- final_df

```


```{r verify-column-names}
#print column names to verify
cat("\n=== FINAL DATAFRAME COLUMNS ===\n")
print(colnames(df))
```


```{r calc-day-of-week}
#Create day of week variables including categorical, sin, and cos variables
df <- df %>%
  mutate(day_of_week = weekdays(as.Date(daterated)),
         day_of_week = factor(day_of_week, levels = c("Sunday", "Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday")),
         day_of_week_sin = sin(2 * pi * as.numeric(day_of_week) / 7),
         day_of_week_cos = cos(2 * pi * as.numeric(day_of_week) / 7))

```

```{r visualize-cycle-events}
# Step 5: Visualize Period Start Dates Over Time with Color-Coded Tiles
periodstartplot <- df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = daterated, y = id, fill = factor(menses))) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(
    title = "Period Start Dates Over Time",
    x = "Date",
    y = "ID",
    fill = "Period Start"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("grey", "red")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

periodstartplot

# Save to data prep subfolder, specify periodstartplot
save_png(plot = periodstartplot, 
         filename = "period_start_dates_heatmap.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder,
         height = 8)  # Keep taller height for heatmap

# print location of saved file
cat("Period start dates heatmap saved\n")

# Step 6: Visualize Ovulation Dates Over Time
ovulationplot <- df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = daterated, y = id, fill = factor(ovtoday))) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(
    title = "Ovulation Dates Over Time",
    x = "Date",
    y = "ID",
    fill = "Ovulation Date"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("grey", "blue")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

# Save to data prep subfolder, specify ovulationplot
save_png(plot = ovulationplot, 
         filename = "ovulation_dates_heatmap.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder,
         height = 8)  # Keep taller height for heatmap

ovulationplot

#df %>% select(id, daterated, menses, ovtoday) %>% View()

##vis_miss(df)

```

```{r horm-import-merge}
# =============================================================================
# HORMONE DATA IMPORT & MERGE
# =============================================================================
# Imports hormone assay results (e2, p4, lh) from Excel file and merges with
# daily survey data. This is a complex merge due to date format inconsistencies.
#
# WHAT IT DOES:
# 1. Loads hormone Excel file with mixed date formats
# 2. Consolidates duplicate columns (date, TubeNumber, hormones)
# 3. Assigns calendar dates to samples using written dates OR calculated dates
# 4. Deduplicates hormone data (one row per person per day)
# 5. Merges with survey data using FULL JOIN
#
# HORMONE VARIABLES:
# - e2: Estradiol (pg/mL) - primary female sex hormone
# - p4: Progesterone (ng/mL) - maintains uterine lining, rises after ovulation
# - lh: Luteinizing Hormone (mIU/mL) - triggers ovulation
#
# DATA COLLECTION METHOD:
# - Participants self-collected saliva samples daily
# - Samples numbered sequentially (TubeNumber: 1, 2, 3...)
# - Some tubes had handwritten dates, others did not
# - All samples batch-analyzed for hormone concentrations
#
# COMPLEX DATE ASSIGNMENT LOGIC (Lines 1274-1301):
# Challenge: Not all tubes have written dates, need to infer collection date
# Strategy: Use TWO methods and prioritize written dates
#
# Method A (parsed_date):
#   - Parse any written date from tube label
#   - Try multiple formats: ymd, mdy, dmy
#   - Example: "05/15/2021" → 2021-05-15
#
# Method B (calculated_date):
#   - Calculate from TubeNumber and study start date
#   - Logic: TubeNumber 1 = Day 1, TubeNumber 5 = Day 5 (start + 4 days)
#   - Requires: study_start date from first survey for each participant
#   - Example: If start = Jan 1, TubeNumber 5 → Jan 5
#
# FINAL DECISION RULE (Line 1298):
#   - Use parsed_date if available (handwritten date)
#   - Fall back to calculated_date if no written date
#   - Rationale: Written dates are more reliable but not always present
#
# DEDUPLICATION (Lines 1307-1314):
# Problem: Multiple hormone measurements for same person-day
# Causes: Re-runs, duplicate entries in lab file
# Solution: Keep first row after sorting by:
#   1. Valid e2 values first (is.na(e2))
#   2. Rows with explicit dates first (is.na(raw_date))
# Rationale: Prioritize complete, explicitly dated measurements
#
# MERGE STRATEGY (Lines 1316-1333):
# - Uses FULL JOIN (not inner or left join)
# - Preserves survey-only rows (have survey but no hormone data)
# - Preserves hormone-only rows (have hormone but no survey data)
# - Creates flag: in_original_survey (TRUE/FALSE)
#
# WHY FULL JOIN:
# - Some days have survey but no saliva sample collected
# - Some days have saliva sample but no survey completed
# - Need to keep both types for maximum data retention
# - Can analyze survey-only or hormone-only subsets later
#
# "GHOST ROWS" (Lines 1345-1346):
# - Rows added solely for hormone data (no survey data that day)
# - Created when participant collected saliva but didn't complete survey
# - Have e2/p4/lh values but NA for all survey variables
# - Useful for hormone trajectory plots even without survey data
#
# DATA RECOVERY (Lines 1341-1342):
# - Counts rows saved by calculated_date method
# - Shows how many samples would have been lost without calculation approach
# - Validates that calculation strategy is adding value
#
# INTERMEDIATE SAVE (Lines 1357-1358):
# - Saves checkpoint: df_with_hormones.rds
# - Allows restarting analysis from this point
# - Useful if subsequent steps fail
#
# KNOWN ISSUES:
# - TODO Line 1377: Need to update pipeline with better cycle date integration
# - Missing hormone data for some ID ranges (noted in comments)
# - Some date calculations may be off by 1 day (check TubeNumber=1 should be start date)
#
# INPUTS:
#   - Excel: ukalc_all_horm_data_20251218.xlsx
#   - df: Survey data with study_start dates
# OUTPUTS:
#   - df: Now includes e2, p4, lh, TubeNumber
#   - Intermediate file: df_with_hormones.rds
# NEW ROWS: May add "ghost rows" for hormone-only days
# =============================================================================

# ==============================================================================
# 1. Load and Clean Hormone Data
# ==============================================================================
# Use read_excel with col_types="text" to safely read mixed data without crashing
rawhormdf <- read_excel(
  "~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/02_datasets/UKALC_HORM/02_data_prep_workspace/ukalc_all_horm_data_20251218.xlsx",
  col_types = "text",           
  .name_repair = "unique_quiet" 
) %>%
  select(-matches("^\\.\\.\\."))

# --- Helper: Safely Merge Duplicate Columns ---
coalesce_pattern <- function(df, pattern, new_name) {
  # Find columns matching the regex pattern
  cols_to_fix <- grep(pattern, names(df), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
  
  if (length(cols_to_fix) == 0) return(df)
  
  # Create new column by taking the first non-NA value from the found columns
  df <- df %>% mutate(!!new_name := coalesce(!!!syms(cols_to_fix)))
  
  # Remove the old columns (unless it's the same name as the new one)
  cols_to_drop <- setdiff(cols_to_fix, new_name)
  if (length(cols_to_drop) > 0) df <- df %>% select(-all_of(cols_to_drop))
  
  return(df)
}

hormdf <- rawhormdf %>%
  # 1. Consolidate Tube Numbers (e.g., "Tube Number", "Tube_Number")
  coalesce_pattern("Tube[_ ]?Number", "TubeNumber") %>%
  
  # 2. Consolidate Dates (Crucial Fix for your issue)
  # This regex "^date$|^daterated$" looks specifically for "date" OR "daterated"
  coalesce_pattern("^date$|^daterated$|Collection[_ ]?Date", "raw_date") %>%
  
  # 3. Consolidate Hormone Columns
  coalesce_pattern("^e2", "e2") %>%
  coalesce_pattern("^p4", "p4") %>%
  coalesce_pattern("^lh", "lh") %>%
  
  # 4. Clean & Format Values
  mutate(
    id = as.character(gsub("[^0-9]", "", id)),
    TubeNumber = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(TubeNumber)),
    across(any_of(c("e2", "p4", "lh")), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.)))
  ) %>%
  
  # 5. Filter: Must have ID and TubeNumber to be useful
  filter(!is.na(id), !is.na(TubeNumber))

# ==============================================================================
# 2. Assign Calendar Dates to Tubes
# ==============================================================================
if (!exists("df")) stop("Main dataframe 'df' must be loaded to calculate dates.")

# A. Calculate Study Start Date (Day 1) for each person from main survey data
study_start_dates <- df %>%
  mutate(daterated = as_date(daterated)) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarize(
    study_start = min(daterated, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# B. Apply Logic: "Written Date" > "Calculated Date"
hormdf <- hormdf %>%
  left_join(study_start_dates, by = "id") %>%
  mutate(
    # Strategy A: Use the written date from the tube (if it exists)
    parsed_date = as_date(parse_date_time(raw_date, orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE)),
    
    # Strategy B: Calculate it based on the Tube Index
    # Logic: If Start Date is Jan 1st, Tube 5 is Jan 5th (Start + 4 days)
    calculated_date = study_start + days(TubeNumber - 1),
    
    # Final Decision: Use parsed if available, otherwise default to calculated
    daterated = coalesce(parsed_date, calculated_date)
  ) %>%
  # Drop rows where we failed to find ANY date (likely ID missing from df)
  filter(!is.na(daterated))

# ==============================================================================
# 3. Deduplicate & Merge
# ==============================================================================

# A. Deduplicate: One hormone row per ID/Day
hormdf_clean <- hormdf %>%
  # Sort preference: Valid e2 values first, then rows that had explicit raw_dates
  arrange(id, daterated, is.na(e2), is.na(raw_date)) %>% 
  group_by(id, daterated) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(id, daterated, TubeNumber, any_of(c("e2", "p4", "lh")))

# B. Prepare Main Data (Flagging original rows)
df_ready <- df %>%
  mutate(
    id = as.character(id), 
    daterated = as_date(daterated),
    in_original_survey = TRUE
  ) %>%
  # Clean out old hormone columns to prevent duplicates
  select(-any_of(c("e2", "p4", "lh", "TubeNumber_Hormone")))

# C. FULL JOIN (Preserves Surveys AND Hormone-Only rows)
final_df <- df_ready %>%
  full_join(hormdf_clean, by = c("id", "daterated")) %>%
  mutate(
    # If 'in_original_survey' is NA, it means this row came from the hormone file
    in_original_survey = replace_na(in_original_survey, FALSE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(id, daterated)


# ===== ADD THIS FIX =====
# Fill Excl_Horm for hormone-only rows from the participant's other rows
cat("\n=== Filling Excl_Horm for hormone-only rows ===\n")

excl_before <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_na = sum(is.na(Excl_Horm))) %>%
  summarise(total_na = sum(n_na)) %>%
  pull(total_na)

cat("Rows with Excl_Horm = NA before fill:", excl_before, "\n")

final_df <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    Excl_Horm = first(na.omit(Excl_Horm)) # Use the participant's Excl_Horm value for all their rows
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

excl_after <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_na = sum(is.na(Excl_Horm))) %>%
  summarise(total_na = sum(n_na)) %>%
  pull(total_na)

cat("Rows with Excl_Horm = NA after fill:", excl_after, "\n")
# ===== END OF FIX =====


# CHECKPOINT 2: After merging hormones into final_df (around line 1950)
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT 2: After Hormone Merge (Before Excl_Horm) ===\n")
hormone_check_2 <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_e2 = sum(!is.na(e2)),
    has_Excl_Horm = any(! is.na(Excl_Horm)),
    Excl_Horm_value = first(Excl_Horm)
  ) %>%
  filter(n_e2 > 0)
cat("IDs with any hormone data:", n_distinct(hormone_check_2$id), "\n")
cat("IDs with >10 hormone obs:", sum(hormone_check_2$n_e2 > 10), "\n")
cat("IDs with Excl_Horm = TRUE:", sum(hormone_check_2$Excl_Horm_value == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")





# Set e2, p4, lh to NA for participants with Excl_Horm = TRUE
final_df <- final_df %>%
  mutate(
    e2 = if_else(Excl_Horm %in% TRUE, NA_real_, e2),
    p4 = if_else(Excl_Horm %in% TRUE, NA_real_, p4),
    lh = if_else(Excl_Horm %in% TRUE, NA_real_, lh)
  )







# CHECKPOINT 3: After applying Excl_Horm (around line 1962)
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT 3: After Excl_Horm Applied ===\n")
hormone_check_3 <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_e2 = sum(!is.na(e2))) %>%
  filter(n_e2 > 0)
cat("IDs with any hormone data:", n_distinct(hormone_check_3$id), "\n")
cat("IDs with >10 hormone obs:", sum(hormone_check_3$n_e2 > 10), "\n")






# Set e2 to NA for id-date combinations indicated by espike1 date or espike2 date
# Note: espike1, espike2, pspike1, pspike2 are already in final_df from earlier join at line 1617-1618
final_df <- final_df %>%
  mutate(
    e2 = if_else(
      (! is.na(espike1) & daterated == espike1) | 
      (!is.na(espike2) & daterated == espike2), 
      NA_real_, 
      e2
    ),
    p4 = if_else(
      (! is.na(pspike1) & daterated == pspike1) | 
      (!is.na(pspike2) & daterated == pspike2), 
      NA_real_, 
      p4
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-espike1, -espike2, -pspike1, -pspike2)




# CHECKPOINT 4: After applying spike date filtering (around line 1970)
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT 4: After Spike Date Filtering ===\n")
hormone_check_4 <- final_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(n_e2 = sum(!is.na(e2))) %>%
  filter(n_e2 > 0)
cat("IDs with any hormone data:", n_distinct(hormone_check_4$id), "\n")
cat("IDs with >10 hormone obs:", sum(hormone_check_4$n_e2 > 10), "\n")

# CHECKPOINT: Verify Excl_Horm is still working (no leakage from spike filtering)
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT: Verify Excl_Horm After Spike Filtering ===\n")
excl_horm_diagnostic <- final_df %>%
  filter(Excl_Horm == TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_with_e2 = sum(!is.na(e2)),
    n_with_p4 = sum(!is.na(p4)),
    n_with_lh = sum(!is.na(lh))
  )
print(excl_horm_diagnostic)
if (any(excl_horm_diagnostic[c("n_with_e2", "n_with_p4", "n_with_lh")] > 0)) {
  warning("WARNING: Excl_Horm=TRUE participants still have hormone values after spike filtering!")
}





# ==============================================================================
# 4. Diagnostics
# ==============================================================================
cat("Final Rows:", nrow(final_df), "\n")

# Count rows recovered by calculation
recovered_rows <- hormdf %>% filter(is.na(parsed_date) & !is.na(calculated_date))
cat("Rows with NO written date that were saved by calculation:", nrow(recovered_rows), "\n")

# Count Ghost Rows (Hormone data with no survey)
ghost_rows <- final_df %>% filter(in_original_survey == FALSE)
cat("New rows added solely for hormone data (no survey):", nrow(ghost_rows), "\n")

# Cleanup flag
final_df <- final_df %>% select(-in_original_survey)

# Assign final_df back to df for consistency with later code
df <- final_df

# ==============================================================================
# 5. View Results
# ==============================================================================
#View(df)
count_rows_ids(df)



names(df)

```

# Calculate aggregate DRSP scores
```{r calc-drsp-aggregates}
# =============================================================================
# DRSP COMPOSITE SCORE CALCULATION (FOR UKALC DIAGNOSTIC DATA)
# =============================================================================
# NOTE: These subscales are calculated here for the UKALC dataset specifically,
# but they are ALSO calculated in UKALC_03_processing_shared.Rmd for the 
# merged ADHD+UKALC dataset to ensure both studies receive identical processing.
# =============================================================================

# set drsp variables to numeric
df <- df %>%
  mutate(across(c("dep", "hopeless", "worthless", "anxious", "mood_swing", "rej_sens", 
                  "angry", "conflicts", "loss_interest", "diff_conc", "tired", 
                  "over_eat", "sp_food_crav", "high_sleep", "diff_sleep", "overwhelm", 
                  "loss_control", "breast_tend", "breast_swell", "headache", 
                  "jm_pain", "loss_prod", "int_relat"), as.numeric))

# Create total DRSP symptom score (means of all items)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(drsp_total = rowMeans(select(., 
      dep, hopeless, worthless, anxious, mood_swing, rej_sens, 
      angry, conflicts, loss_interest, diff_conc, tired, 
      over_eat, sp_food_crav, high_sleep, diff_sleep, overwhelm, 
      loss_control, breast_tend, breast_swell, headache, 
      jm_pain, loss_prod, int_relat
  ), na.rm = TRUE))

# Create "drsp_distress" - dep hope worth anx ang loss_interest overwhelm loss_control
df <- df %>%
  mutate(drsp_distress = rowMeans(select(., 
      dep, hopeless, worthless, anxious, overwhelm, loss_control), na.rm = TRUE))

# create drsp_labilsocsx - mood_swing, rej_sens, conflicts,int_relat, angry
df <- df %>%
  mutate(drsp_labilsocsx = rowMeans(select(., mood_swing, rej_sens, conflicts, int_relat, angry), na.rm = TRUE))

# create drsp_breastpain - breast_tend, breast_swell
df <- df %>%
  mutate(drsp_breastpain = rowMeans(select(., breast_tend, breast_swell), na.rm = TRUE))

# create drsp_eatsx - over_eat, sp_food_crav
df <- df %>%
  mutate(drsp_eatsx = rowMeans(select(., over_eat, sp_food_crav), na.rm = TRUE))

# create drsp_lowarousal - tired, diff_conc, diff_sleep, high_sleep
df <- df %>%
  mutate(drsp_lowarousal = rowMeans(select(., tired, diff_conc, diff_sleep, high_sleep, loss_interest, loss_prod, headache), na.rm = TRUE))

```

```{r winsorize-hormones}
library(dplyr)

# 1. Define a simple helper function to cap the top 5%
winsor_upper <- function(x, prob = 0.95) {
  # If all data is missing, return as is to avoid errors
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(x)
  
  # Calculate the cutoff
  limit <- quantile(x, probs = prob, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Replace values above the limit with the limit
  x[x > limit & !is.na(x)] <- limit
  return(x)
}

# 2. Apply it within-person
df <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    e2_w = winsor_upper(e2, 0.95),
    p4_w = winsor_upper(p4, 0.95),
    lh_w = winsor_upper(lh, 0.95)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Enforce Excl_Horm exclusion on winsorized hormone variables (e2_w, p4_w, lh_w)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    e2_w = if_else(Excl_Horm %in% TRUE, NA_real_, e2_w),
    p4_w = if_else(Excl_Horm %in% TRUE, NA_real_, p4_w),
    lh_w = if_else(Excl_Horm %in% TRUE, NA_real_, lh_w)
  )

# Verify exclusion enforcement
cat("\n=== CHECKPOINT: After Winsorization ===\n")
excl_horm_check <- df %>%
  filter(Excl_Horm == TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_with_e2 = sum(!is.na(e2)),
    n_with_e2_w = sum(!is.na(e2_w)),
    n_with_p4 = sum(!is.na(p4)),
    n_with_p4_w = sum(!is.na(p4_w)),
    n_with_lh = sum(!is.na(lh)),
    n_with_lh_w = sum(!is.na(lh_w))
  )
print(excl_horm_check)
if (any(excl_horm_check > 0)) {
  warning("WARNING: Excl_Horm=TRUE participants still have hormone values after winsorization!")
}
```

```{r note-derived-metrics-moved}
# =============================================================================
# NOTE: DERIVED METRICS CALCULATION MOVED TO UKALC_03_processing_shared.Rmd
# =============================================================================
# 
# Previous versions of this file calculated derived variables here (.d, .zd, 
# .szd, .roll, .log, .m, .sd). This caused problems when merging with ADHD data
# because ADHD observations never received these transformations.
#
# NOW: This file outputs CLEAN data only (no derived variables)
# - Output: data/ukalc_YYYYMMDD_clean.rds
# - Next step: UKALC_02 merges clean datasets
# - Final step: UKALC_03 applies ALL processing to merged data
#
# This ensures both UKALC and ADHD observations receive identical processing.
# =============================================================================
```

```{r remove-dupes}
df <- df %>%
  distinct(id, daterated, .keep_all = TRUE)
```

```{r how-many-drinking-ids}
#How many unique ids are in the dataset? 
ids_in_am_dataset <- n_distinct(df$id) # 83

# How many unique participants (factor var id) reported drinking at least once? 
unique_drinkers <- n_distinct(df$id[df$drink_today == "Yes"]) # 79

# Print out each id with the number of times that each id reported drinking
df %>%
  filter(drink_today == "Yes") %>%
  count(id) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

table(df$drink_today)

# use cat to print out the number of unique ids and unique drinkers
cat("Number of unique ids in the dataset: ", ids_in_am_dataset, "\n")


```

```{r visualize-drinks-fourplus}

p_drink_today <- df %>% 
  filter(!is.na(drink_today)) %>% 
  # CHANGE 1: Wrap drink_today in as.factor() to make it discrete
  ggplot(aes(x = daterated, y = id, fill = as.factor(drink_today))) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(title = "Drinking Across the Study by ID",
       x = "Date",
       y = "ID",
       fill = "Drinking Today") +
  # CHANGE 2: Quote the numbers ("1" and "0") so R recognizes them as names
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Yes" = "blue", "No" = "lightgray")) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

print(p_drink_today)

# Save the plot
save_png(plot = p_drink_today, 
         filename = "drink_today_heatmap.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder,
         height = 8)  # Keep taller height for heatmap



p_fourplustoday <- df %>% 
  filter(!is.na(fourplustoday)) %>% 
  # CHANGE 1: Wrap fourplustoday in as.factor() to make it discrete
  ggplot(aes(x = daterated, y = id, fill = as.factor(fourplustoday))) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(title = "Drinking 4+ Drinks/Day Across the Study by ID",
       x = "Date",
       y = "ID",
       fill = "Drinking 4+ Today") +
  # CHANGE 2: Quote the numbers ("1" and "0") so R recognizes them as names
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Yes" = "blue", "No" = "lightgray")) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

print(p_fourplustoday)

# Save the plot
save_png(plot = p_fourplustoday, 
         filename = "fourplustoday_heatmap.png",
         folder = data_prep_folder,
         height = 8)  # Keep taller height for heatmap

```

# Save cycledata and save as traits
```{r merge-cycledata-traits}

availablecycledata <- cycledata_check(df, c("dep"))

# format id as same format for merging 
availablecycledata <- availablecycledata$by_id %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id))

# merge drink_cycledata into df
df <- df %>%
  left_join(availablecycledata, by = "id")

# check merge
cat("Cycledata merged into df. Here are the new columns from availablecycledata:\n")
new_cycledata_cols <- setdiff(names(availablecycledata), names(df))
print(new_cycledata_cols)

```




```{r count rows and ids}
count_rows_ids(df)
```

```{r data N details}

# Filter the data for non-missing e2 values and count unique ids
non_missing_E2 <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(e2))

non_missing_E2_count <- n_distinct(non_missing_E2$id)
non_missing_E2_ids <- unique(non_missing_E2$id)

# Get IDs with missing e2 values
missing_E2_ids <- setdiff(unique(df$id), non_missing_E2_ids)

# Print results for non-missing and missing e2 values
cat("Number of people WITH usable hormones:", non_missing_E2_count, "\n",
    "The id values are:", paste(non_missing_E2_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n\n",
    "Number of people WITHOUT usable hormone data:", length(missing_E2_ids), "\n",
    "Their id values are:", paste(missing_E2_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n\n",
    "In addition, these ids were removed due to a lack of complete data:", 
    paste(ids_w_no_menses_onset, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

# Filter the data for non-missing survey values and count unique ids
non_missing_survey <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(drank_alcohol))

non_missing_survey_count <- n_distinct(non_missing_survey$id)
non_missing_survey_ids <- unique(non_missing_survey$id)

# Get IDs with missing survey values
missing_survey_ids <- setdiff(unique(df$id), non_missing_survey_ids)

# Print results for non-missing and missing survey values
cat("Number of people WITH any surveys:", non_missing_survey_count, "\n",
    "Their id values are:", paste(non_missing_survey_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n\n",
    "Number of people WITHOUT any surveys:", length(missing_survey_ids), "\n",
    "Their id values are:", paste(missing_survey_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

# Find the intersection of available hormone data and available survey data
intersection_ids <- intersect(non_missing_E2_ids, non_missing_survey_ids)
intersection_count <- length(intersection_ids)

# Print result for the intersection of hormone and survey data
cat("Number of people WITH both usable hormone and any survey data:", intersection_count, "\n",
    "Their id values are:", paste(intersection_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

# Count the non-missing observations of drink_today and e2 for people with both hormone and survey data
non_missing_intersection <- df %>%
  filter(id %in% intersection_ids, !is.na(drink_today), !is.na(e2))

non_missing_intersection_count <- nrow(non_missing_intersection)

# Print the result for non-missing observations of drink_today and e2
cat("Number of non-missing observations of drink_today and e2 for people with both hormone and survey data:", non_missing_intersection_count, "\n")

# Create a list of IDs with the number of days with both e2 and DRSP
usable_days_list <- non_missing_intersection %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(usable_days = n()) %>%
  arrange(usable_days)

# Print the list of IDs with the number of days in words
cat("List of IDs with the number of days having both e2 and DRSP (sorted by number of observations ascending):", "\n")
apply(usable_days_list, 1, function(row) {
  cat("id", row["id"], "has", row["usable_days"], "hormone-survey pairs.", "\n")
})

```

```{r create-days-since-enrollment}
# Create a variable called days_since_enrollment that counts the number of days since the first day of data collection (daterated) for each participant (id) in df
df <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(days_since_enrollment = daterated - min(daterated))

# Create a variable that counts the number of rows per id and saves it as a trait variable (repeating within each id)
df <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(id_daycount = n())

hist(df$id_daycount)
```

```{r data-overlap-horm-surv-cyc, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# ---- Plot Overlap of Available Data  ----        
# Plot days in study, nonmissing survey observations, and e2 observations per id 
#TODO need to add hormone data into this and redo
library(gridExtra)
# Count the total number of "Days in Study" (total observations per ID)
df_count <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarize(id_daycount = n())

# Count the number of non-missing observations per ID for the variable drink_today_am
df_nonmissing_count <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarize(number_of_observations = sum(!is.na(drink_today)))

# Count the number of non-missing observations for the variable e2
df_e2_nonmissing_count <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarize(e2_nonmissing = sum(!is.na(e2)))

# Count the number of non-missing observations for the variable dep
df_dep_nonmissing_count <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarize(dep_nonmissing = sum(!is.na(dep)))

#### Handle Outliers ONLY for "Days in Study" ####

# Outlier removal for "Days in Study"
Q1 <- quantile(df_count$id_daycount, 0.25)
Q3 <- quantile(df_count$id_daycount, 0.75)
IQR <- Q3 - Q1
lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

df_no_outliers <- df_count %>%
  filter(id_daycount >= lower_bound & id_daycount <= upper_bound)

# remove id 123 from df_dep_nonmissing_count 
df_dep_nonmissing_count <- df_dep_nonmissing_count %>%
  filter(id != 123)

#### Determine Y-Axis Limits ####
y_min <- min(df_no_outliers$id_daycount, df_nonmissing_count$number_of_observations, df_e2_nonmissing_count$e2_nonmissing, df_dep_nonmissing_count$dep_nonmissing, na.rm = TRUE)
y_max <- max(df_no_outliers$id_daycount, df_nonmissing_count$number_of_observations, df_e2_nonmissing_count$e2_nonmissing, df_dep_nonmissing_count$dep_nonmissing, na.rm = TRUE)

#### Plots ####

# Plot "Days in Study" per ID (with outliers removed)
plot1_no_outliers <- ggplot(df_no_outliers, aes(x = factor(1), y = id_daycount, label = id)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.5) +  # Violin plot to show distribution
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Boxplot inside the violin
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 2, color = "blue", alpha = 0.7) +  # Dots for raw data
  geom_text(aes(label = id), hjust = -0.1, size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +  # Add ID labels
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Days in Study", title = "Days in Study per ID") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank()) +
  ylim(y_min, y_max)

# Plot "Number of Observations" for drink_today_am (without removing outliers)
plot2_no_outliers <- ggplot(df_nonmissing_count, aes(x = factor(1), y = number_of_observations, label = id)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.5) +  # Violin plot to show distribution
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Boxplot inside the violin
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 2, color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +  # Dots for raw data
  geom_text(aes(label = id), hjust = -0.1, size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +  # Add ID labels
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Alcohol Survey Days", title = "AM Surveys per ID") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank()) +
  ylim(y_min, y_max)

# Plot "Number of non-missing e2 observations" (without removing outliers)
plot3_no_outliers <- ggplot(df_e2_nonmissing_count, aes(x = factor(1), y = e2_nonmissing, label = id)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightcoral", alpha = 0.5) +  # Violin plot to show distribution
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Boxplot inside the violin
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 2, color = "red", alpha = 0.7) +  # Dots for raw data
  geom_text(aes(label = id), hjust = -0.1, size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +  # Add ID labels
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of Hormone Days", title = "Hormone Days per ID") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank()) +
  ylim(y_min, y_max)

# Plot "Number of non-missing dep observations" (without removing outliers)
plot4_no_outliers <- ggplot(df_dep_nonmissing_count, aes(x = factor(1), y = dep_nonmissing, label = id)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "orange", alpha = 0.5) +  # Violin plot to show distribution
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Boxplot inside the violin
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 2, color = "red", alpha = 0.7) +  # Dots for raw data
  geom_text(aes(label = id), hjust = -0.1, size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +  # Add ID labels
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of DRSP Days", title = "PM Surveys per ID") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank()) +
  ylim(y_min, y_max)

# Combine the three plots on one page using grid.arrange from gridExtra
observations_plot <- grid.arrange(plot1_no_outliers, plot2_no_outliers, plot3_no_outliers, plot4_no_outliers, ncol = 4)

# Save the combined plot to a file with the current date
save_png(plot = observations_plot, 
         filename = paste0("N_observations_plot_", current_date, ".png"),
         folder = data_prep_folder,
         width = 20,  # Keep wider for 3-column layout
         height = 8)



```




```{r print variable names}

names(df)

```





##### END OF DATA PREP 

```{r save-prepped-dataset-box}

write.csv(df, paste0("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/03_analytic_projects/UKALC-Primary ms/02_datasets_output/ukalc_", current_date, "_clean.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Save as .rds
saveRDS(df, paste0("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/UKALC/03_analytic_projects/UKALC-Primary ms/02_datasets_output/ukalc_", current_date, "_clean.rds"))
```


```{r save-prepped-dataset-data-folder}
# output a copy to the data folder
write.csv(df, paste0("data/ukalc_", current_date, "_clean.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
# Save as .rds
saveRDS(df, paste0("data/ukalc_", current_date, "_clean.rds"))

cat("\n✓ Clean dataset saved (no derived variables).\n")
cat("  Output: data/ukalc_", current_date, "_clean.rds\n", sep="")
cat("  Next step: Run UKALC_02_merge_adhd.Rmd to merge with ADHD data.\n")
cat("  Then: Run UKALC_03_processing_shared.Rmd to create derived variables.\n")
```

```{r placeholder-empty}

```
